Meta-architectural framing surfaced during dogfooding-perspective discussion 2026-05-10 + load-bearing under DEC-0014 because the consumer the substrate is built for cannot validate substrate operability via build/check/test — only by attempting actual arc dispatch through the substrate's surface.

evidence.reference: 22 enumerated gaps + meta-pattern (pi tool surface systematically lags JS export surface; ~40% query coverage). Under DEC-0014 every gap becomes load-bearing because no bash/read/write fallback exists for the main LLM.

DEC-0014 (enacted 2026-05-10) establishes harness-confined main LLM as canonical execution surface. Implies the substrate's completion criterion is operability under that constraint, not isolated tool/primitive existence.

8 typed-file primitives from FGAP-019/020 closure are JS exports with no agent-reachable tool wrappers. Tool-existence completion proxy treated FGAP-020 as closed; under DEC-0014 lens the architectural-symmetry deliverable is invisible to the consumer.

Subcommand-only surfaces (/project view, /project lens-curate, /project install, /project add-work) per the gap audit are unreachable by the harness-confined main LLM under DEC-0014 — the build/check/test gate at Step 8 closure passed without surfacing this incompleteness.

FGAP-026 (config-driven bootstrap), FGAP-024 (write-authority enforcement), FGAP-005 (state-machine transitions), FGAP-002/003/007/008/009/010/014/015 (other identified gaps) all surface or amplify under the dogfood-completeness criterion. Without FGAP-028's framing, each is enumerated in isolation; under FGAP-028, they collectively define what 'arc-dispatchable substrate' means.

gaps.impact: Without dogfood-completeness as the criterion, substrate-arc work can ship as 'complete' while structurally inoperable for the consumer the substrate is built for. Concrete near-term consequence: v0.25.0 release (Task #12 / Step 10) could ship with all currently-enumerated FGAPs closed, all 4 packages tests green, all docs aligned, and a fresh-repo harness-confined main LLM still cannot author + execute its own arc because (a) filter-block-items doesn't exist (90 issues, can't query); (b) workflow-execute is subcommand-only (can't dispatch out-of-substrate work); (c) arc-tracking blocks (roadmap/phase/tasks/relations/config) have no concrete instances; (d) per-item read returns location not payload; etc. Step 9 (adversarial audit) and Step 10 (release) currently lack the dogfood-dispatch gate that would catch this. The rebuild arc could declare itself complete and ship a v0.25.0 npm publish that immediately surfaces as inoperable on first user dispatch.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add dogfood-completeness as the explicit substrate-arc completion criterion alongside build/check/test/skills/commit/release sequence in CLAUDE.md Completion Sequence. Operationalize: every arc-completion release runs a 'harness-confined dispatch dry-run' — synthetic main-LLM-as-tool-only invocation of a representative arc (e.g., file a new FGAP via append-block-item with DispatchContext, transition its status via update-block-item, dispatch a workflow that exercises a JIT-agent build, mark phase complete via update on roadmap/phase block) — using only extension tools, no bash/read/write fallback. If the dry-run fails because of missing tools/primitives, those become P0 closure items before release. Step 9 (adversarial audit, Task #11) gains a dogfood-dispatch test gate as part of its protocol — fresh-context audit subagent dispatched with HARNESS-CONFINED tool surface, asked to perform representative arc work, surfaces gaps as findings. Step 10 (release, Task #12) is conditioned on Step 9's dogfood-dispatch passing. Future arcs scoped against dogfood-completeness as precondition. Couples to FGAP-026 (resolver + bootstrap pointer enables harness-confined operability), tool-surface gap audit (concrete tool deliverables block dispatch capability), DEC-0014 (defines the canonical execution surface dogfood measures against). The 7-phase FGAP-026 closure plan needs re-sequencing under this criterion: high-impact tool gaps (filter-block-items, read-config, read-schema, workflow-execute) pulled INTO Phase 1 so subsequent phases become harness-confined-dispatchable; arc-tracking blocks (roadmap/phase/tasks/relations/config) authored mid-arc as both the dogfood-dispatch substrate AND the arc-tracking record. Per mandate-007 surfaced as substrate-canon, not architectural-debt-deferred.

gaps.title: Bidirectional scoped traversal primitive missing — walk-ancestors + walk-by-relation tools
gaps.description: DEC-0017 (work-unit context composed at dispatch time) requires bidirectional traversal of closure-table edges per relation_type. Today only project-walk-descendants exists; reverse traversal is not exposed. find-references (cross-block reverse-index) gap from the tool-surface audit overlaps this. The gather-execution-context primitive (FGAP-031) cannot exist without bidirectional traversal as foundational layer.

evidence.reference: walkDescendants exists; no walkAncestors. Reverse traversal logic is per-relation_type DFS over relations.json edges; primitive shape is symmetric to walkDescendants but reverse-direction.
8-rank consolidated impact ranking position 5: project-walk-ancestors. Position 10: find-references (cross-block reverse-index). Both load-bearing for DEC-0017 work-unit context composition.

gaps.impact: Without bidirectional traversal, work-unit context cannot be gathered — backward edges from a unit to its constraining/motivating context are invisible. Under DEC-0014/0016 dogfood-completeness lens, work-unit dispatch is undispatchable because the orchestrator cannot ask 'what context informs this unit'. Cascades through DEC-0017 dependencies: FGAP-031 (gather primitive) needs FGAP-029; FGAP-032 (contextBlocks item-level selectivity) needs FGAP-031.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add walk-ancestors(childId, relationType) tool wrapping a new walkAncestors JS export in project-context.ts (symmetric to walkDescendants; reverse-direction DFS over relations edges of the given relation_type, cycle-safe via visited-set). Add walk-by-relation(itemId, relationType, direction: 'in'|'out'|'both') generalized tool covering both directions in one primitive. Item-level scoping per applicability predicate (FGAP-010) optional at first; can layer in later. Cascades into pi-context's project-context.ts SDK + new tool registrations in index.ts.

gaps.title: Per-work-unit-kind context contract substrate missing — no way to declare which relation_types compose the execution bundle for each unit kind
gaps.description: DEC-0017 specifies per-unit-kind context contract: implementation tasks need conventions + acceptance + architecture-touched; decision-making tasks need rationale + alternatives + related-decisions; verification tasks need verification-criteria + evidence-rules + items-under-verification. Today the substrate has NO mechanism to declare per-unit-kind which relation_types compose the execution bundle. gather-execution-context (FGAP-031) cannot dispatch correctly without reading the contract for the unit-kind being executed.

evidence.reference: No context-contract.schema.json; no schema declaring per-kind bundle composition. Existing schemas (config, tasks, phase, decisions, etc.) carry the unit-kind data but no kind-to-bundle mapping.

ConfigBlock declares lenses[] (composition lens machinery from FGAP-012 closure exists); the context-contract could plausibly encode AS composition lenses keyed by unit-kind, OR as a new context-contract block kind. Decision deferred to FGAP-030 closure work.

gaps.impact: Without per-unit-kind context contract, gather-execution-context cannot know what to gather for a given unit. Either gather returns everything (bundle pollution; defeats relevance scoping) or returns nothing (no contract = nothing to bundle). The substrate has no extensibility path for new unit-kinds without code changes — same antipattern as FGAP-013's pre-DEC-0013 hardcoded status vocabulary.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Two coherent implementation shapes — closure work decides which: (a) NEW BLOCK KIND: context-contract.json with schema declaring per-kind { unitKind: string, bundle_relation_types: { in: string[], out: string[], depth: number, applicability_predicate?: string }[] }. Authoring contract per unit-kind is opt-in via /context install (default contracts ship in registry/blocks/). (b) COMPOSITION-LENS ENCODING: context contracts encoded in config.lenses[] as kind='execution-context-bundle' lenses keyed by unit-kind. Reuses FGAP-012 closure machinery. Either is DEC-0017 compliant. (b) is simpler but less explicit; (a) is more explicit but adds a block kind. Closure work picks one + documents rationale.

gaps.title: Composition-of-views work-unit-driven primitive missing — gather-execution-context(unitId, kind) tool and JS export
gaps.description: DEC-0017 specifies a single substrate operation that reads the unit + reads its context contract (FGAP-030) + walks declared relation_types bidirectionally (FGAP-029) + composes the result as one structured bundle returned as one tool return. Today no such primitive exists; orchestrator must do N+1 reads + ad-hoc composition. Under DEC-0014/0016 + FGAP-028 dogfood-completeness, this is the load-bearing primitive between substrate and JIT-agent execution.

evidence.reference: loadLensView composes lens projection but is lens-driven, not work-unit-driven. resolveComposition (FGAP-012 closure) composes member items but doesn't apply per-unit-kind context contract or work-unit-driven traversal. Closest existing surface; insufficient for work-unit bundling.

22-row gap table does not yet enumerate gather-execution-context — DEC-0017 surfaces this as a NEW gap not previously enumerated. Under DEC-0017 it joins as P1 candidate alongside filter-block-items / read-config / read-schema.

gaps.impact: Without gather-execution-context, work-unit dispatch under DEC-0014/0016 requires the orchestrator to: (1) read the unit; (2) read the contract for its kind; (3) walk N relation_types bidirectionally; (4) read each related item; (5) compose the bundle. That's 5+N tool calls per unit dispatch, with hand-rolled composition logic. With the primitive: 1 tool call returns the composed bundle. The N+1 pattern defeats DEC-0014/0016 + FGAP-028 dogfood-dispatchability.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Author gather-execution-context(unitId: string, kind: string): ContextBundle as JS export in packages/pi-context/src/ (likely new module: execution-context.ts, OR within lens-view.ts as work-unit-driven composition). Pi tool wrapper in index.ts: project-gather-execution-context (or context-gather-execution-context post-rename per FGAP-026 Phase 5). Body composes: (1) readBlock(cwd, unit's block) + filter to unitId; (2) read context contract for kind via FGAP-030 mechanism; (3) for each declared relation_type in contract: walkAncestors + walkDescendants (FGAP-029) within depth bound; (4) resolve each reached itemId to full payload via batch resolve (resolve-items-by-id from gap audit); (5) optionally apply applicability predicate (FGAP-010) for relevance filtering; (6) return ContextBundle = { unit, perRelationType: { [rt]: ItemRecord[] }, traversal_depth, scoped_at }.

gaps.title: Item-level contextBlocks selectivity missing in pi-jit-agents — currently injects whole blocks, not work-unit-driven item selections
gaps.description: DEC-0017 specifies dispatch-time injection: agents receive context bundles as composed inputs, not as N whole-block dumps. Today pi-jit-agents' contextBlocks injection mechanism (DEC-0008 typed contextBlocks form) injects WHOLE BLOCKS — agent's compileAgent template gets the entire decisions.json under {{ _decisions }}, not just the decisions reached via gather-execution-context for THIS work-unit. Bundle from FGAP-031 is structured but injection is unstructured-dump.

evidence.reference: compileAgent reads contextBlocks declarations + injects whole block content under {{ _<name> }} variables. No mechanism to inject item-level selections driven by a work-unit's gather-execution-context output. Per the runtime-step-context analysis (analysis/2026-04-15-runtime-step-context.md) this gap was named earlier as 'agent spec must declare contextBlocks with item-level selectivity'.

AgentSpec.contextBlocks current shape per DEC-0008: typed object form { name, item?, focus?, depth? } — item field exists at type level but is per-block-static, not per-dispatch-derived from a work-unit gather.

gaps.impact: Bundle composition (FGAP-031) returns structured per-relation-type item lists, but injection collapses them back to whole-block dumps in the agent's compiled prompt. Agents receive irrelevant context (sibling items not reached via the work-unit's relation walk) + miss work-unit-specific context (the per-relation-type structure). Defeats DEC-0017's selectivity intent.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Extend AgentSpec.contextBlocks shape to support work-unit-driven gather: { name, gather_for_unit?: { unitId, kind } } — when gather_for_unit is present, compileAgent calls gather-execution-context(unitId, kind) and injects the bundle as { _<name>_bundle: ContextBundle } variable. Templates reference _<block>_bundle.perRelationType.<relation_type>[] for selective rendering. Existing per-item macros (per the macros-as-prompt-substrate work) consume the bundle's per-relation-type collections. Backward compatible: existing { name, item?, focus?, depth? } usage unchanged; gather_for_unit is additive.

gaps.title: No substrate block for communication-style preferences (orchestrator-side concern; lives only in memory layer)
gaps.description: Communication-style preferences (e.g. bullets-not-walls heuristic from feedback_communications_heuristic.md just persisted) live only in the orchestrator-side memory layer at ~/.claude/projects/<this-project>/memory/feedback_*.md. There is no canonical substrate block for communication preferences — no block_kind, no schema, no block-api access. Cross-session continuity relies on memory load + LLM honoring the rule. The same anti-LLM-performance reasoning that put DEC-0018 in substrate canon (so LLMs choosing not to read memory cannot miss it) applies to communication-style preferences. Communication style governs orchestrator output shape across every interaction; missing-from-substrate means future sessions/agents can ignore via memory-skip OR see only stale snapshots.

evidence.reference: Communication heuristic just filed only at memory layer; no substrate mirror
No communication-style schema exists in registry; no block_kind declared
POC had to use native fs + ad-hoc YAML frontmatter parser specifically because feedback layer is outside substrate — same gap shape recurring

gaps.impact: Communication-style preferences are not queryable via block-api / projectState / readBlock. Cross-session continuity depends on memory layer reads only. Agents dispatched without memory access (e.g. JIT agents per DEC-0014, JIT agents in pi-jit-agents harness-confined dispatch dry-run per FGAP-028) cannot retrieve the communication heuristic; their output style drifts from project canon. Same architectural debt class as DEC-0018 was for verification gates: insufficient layering means LLMs can opt out by not reading.
gaps.proposed_resolution: File new block_kind communication-style (or broader: orchestrator-style, agent-style — covering output discipline, hedging policies, brief-construction preferences, etc.). Schema fields per item: id, title, scope (orchestrator|subagent|user-facing), rule, examples, applies_to (which dispatch types), enforcement (instruction|surface), references. Add to registry/schemas/ + registry/blocks/. Cascade: subagent brief composer (build-subagent-preamble.ts) reads via block-api like it reads decisions + feedback. Memory layer entry (feedback_communications_heuristic.md) becomes mirror, not source-of-truth. Same pattern as DEC-0018 substrate-canon-not-just-memory persistence. Could include broader scope: orchestrator-style block also covers no-judgment-vocabulary, no-throat-clearing, depth-matching, etc., as separate items rather than one monolithic style entry.

gaps.title: Orchestrator-side intermediates (Explore agent outputs, brief-composition logs, investigation queries) lack substrate block kinds — markdown-output workflow is the load-bearing intermediate format
gaps.description: Orchestrator-side scripts under scripts/orchestrator/* produce + consume intermediates as markdown files in /tmp (e.g. /tmp/explore-c3-fixtures-v2.md Site Inventory table, /tmp/import-chains-pi-workflows.md per-test-file reach registry, /tmp/c3-impl-brief.md composed brief). Downstream composers (build-implementation-brief.ts) re-extract sections from these markdown files via extract-markdown-section.ts (regex on header text). The shape of every intermediate is structured data (per-row tables, key-value pairs) that would be better-served as substrate blocks with typed schemas, AJV validation, block-api access, and cross-session continuity. Markdown-as-load-bearing-intermediate is the failure surface that produced the C.3 v2 explore-agent prose-summary contradiction (executive summary aggregated table cells incorrectly; cells were sound but summary drifted). Same architectural class as FGAP-033 (communication-style needs substrate block) and the unfiled gaps for mandates substrate-canon home + feedback substrate-canon home — orchestrator-side concerns living outside substrate.

evidence.reference: Generic markdown section extractor; uses regex on header text (line 65). Brittle to header rewrites; no schema validation.

Explore agent's Site Inventory table emitted as markdown; downstream consumer (build-implementation-brief.ts:99) regex-extracts cells. Aggregation hallucination observed: executive summary contradicted detail rows.
extract-test-import-chains.ts output is structured per-file reach data emitted as markdown table; would be queryable substrate if filed via block-api.
Composer reads explore report by markdown-section name; brittle to header changes + cannot validate cell shape.

gaps.impact: Markdown-as-load-bearing-intermediate fails on six axes: (1) hallucination drift — LLM aggregations of table cells contradict the cells (C.3 v2 incident); (2) re-derivability — /tmp/ files lose provenance; cannot reconstruct what substrate-state generated them; (3) cross-session continuity — /tmp/ files vanish on shell restart; future sessions cannot query past dispatches; (4) composability — extract-markdown-section regex on header text is brittle; refactoring header text breaks downstream; (5) validation — markdown table cells are unvalidated; wrong-shape rows pass silently; (6) programmatic consumers — every consumer has its own ad-hoc parser. Substrate block_kinds with typed schemas + AJV validation + block-api access close all six. Six block_kinds elided: cascade-target / audit-result / arc-cross-ref / import-chain / investigation / brief-history.
gaps.proposed_resolution: File six new block_kinds in packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/: cascade-target.schema.json (per-site cascade table rows: file/line/helper-or-inline/tmpdir-contents/first-resolver-cascading-call/classification/justification); audit-result.schema.json (pattern + hit-count + per-hit classification); arc-cross-ref.schema.json (concern-source pointer to existing arc planning); import-chain.schema.json (per-file direct + transitive pi-context reach registry); investigation.schema.json (id + question + target + status + results-pointer for Explore dispatches); brief-history.schema.json (composer-run log: extractors + params + substrate-state SHA at composition). Cascade: each affected scripts/orchestrator/* script writes via file-block-item.ts (or programmatically via block-api); downstream composers replace extract-markdown-section.ts calls with readBlock + filter. Markdown-rendering becomes display-only (substrate is canonical; markdown is a view). Same dual-direction pattern as DEC-0019: filing each block_kind surfaces additional gaps in macros library + per-item rendering; closures feed back to REVIEW-001 + tool-surface gap audit.

gaps.title: DEC-0015 violation: writeBootstrapPointer carries hardcoded .project default; initProject hardcodes .project literal
gaps.description: Two surfaces hardcode the substrate-dir name '.project' in direct violation of DEC-0015 (config drives substrate location, permanently — no hardcoded substrate-dir paths anywhere). (1) packages/pi-context/src/project-dir.ts:162 — writeBootstrapPointer signature has parameter default: `contextDir: string = PROJECT_DIR` where PROJECT_DIR is the deprecated '.project' constant. (2) packages/pi-context/src/index.ts:237 — initProject() calls writeBootstrapPointer(cwd, '.project') with a string literal. Both surfaces synthesize the substrate-dir name from code rather than receiving it from the user. DEC-0015 explicitly rejects default-as-fallback as 'hardcode-dressed-as-default'. The JSDoc at project-dir.ts:147-152 frames the default as 'transitional bridge for self-bootstrapping callers (initProject); future /context init ceremony will prompt the user' — that framing is LLM-rationalized deferral pattern, not enactment of DEC-0015.

evidence.reference: writeBootstrapPointer(cwd: string, contextDir: string = PROJECT_DIR): void — signature default is the violation
JSDoc framing the default as transitional bridge — accepts violation rather than fixing it
writeBootstrapPointer(cwd, '.project') — initProject hardcodes the literal
DEC-0015 permanent canon: NO hardcoded substrate-dir paths anywhere; default-as-fallback explicitly rejected

gaps.impact: Foundational DEC-0015 violation lives at the bootstrap surface — the very primitive that exists to enforce config-driven substrate location is itself hardcoded. Fresh repos calling /project init silently default to .project rather than prompting the user; this contradicts the Phase 6 user-prompt ceremony before it ships. Every Phase-1.2 cascade landed on top of this foundation — the resolver layer is config-driven but the bootstrap-write layer is not. Closing FGAP-026 without fixing FGAP-035 would mark substrate canon enacted while the actual canon-violation persists at the foundational write surface.
gaps.proposed_resolution: (1) Remove parameter default from writeBootstrapPointer signature — `contextDir: string` becomes required. (2) Change initProject signature to require contextDir from caller (passed as param OR obtained from a prompt). (3) Rewrite project-dir.ts:147-152 JSDoc to remove default-as-feature framing. (4) Remove or update PROJECT_DIR/SCHEMAS_DIR @deprecated exports per remaining cascade need. (5) Test sites (168) already pass '.project' explicitly — no test-side changes needed for the signature fix. (6) Determine initProject UX: pass contextDir from /project init subcommand arg OR prompt user via pi.sendMessage follow-up turn (Phase 6 design surface, brought forward).

gaps.title: Missing canonical script: compile-task-context.ts — implementer task-block hand-authored bypasses DEC-0019 dual-surface discipline
gaps.description: scripts/orchestrator/ has compile-preamble-context.ts (mandates + DEC canon + tool-surface preamble), compile-explore-context.ts (composes Explore-agent context; instructs agent to write report to specified path), and compile-implementation-context.ts (composes implementation-agent context from preamble + substrate-state + section-spec + cascade-table + task-template). It lacks a compile-task-context.ts script that programmatically renders the implementer task-block (read-list + apply-order + concrete-edits + commit-spec + STOP triggers + report-back format) from substrate inputs (TASK-NNN id, explore-report path, commit-subject-template). Without it, every implementation-agent context requires hand-authored /tmp/task-template-*.md. Hand-authored content (a) drifts in shape across dispatches, (b) bypasses mandate-005, (c) violates DEC-0019 dual-surface discipline (scripts/orchestrator/ is the Claude-Code-side ergonomics surface; hand-authoring is the parallel ungated path), (d) embeds orchestrator-side guesswork rather than reading the canonical TASK-NNN acceptance_criteria from substrate.

evidence.reference: exists — Explore-agent context composer; writes report to --output path via agent
exists — implementation-agent context composer; takes --task-template as @file (assumed pre-authored)
preamble composer used by both compile-*-context.ts
hand-authored task-template this turn — direct evidence of the gap
DEC-0019 — scripts/orchestrator/* serve as canonical test surface + executable specs; hand-authoring per-task templates is the gap-shaped surface DEC-0019 names

gaps.impact: Every implementation-class subagent dispatch requires hand-authoring the task-template. Across N tasks this means N hand-authored files with no shape canonicalization. Orchestrator-side authoring of task-templates also separates implementation specs from substrate (task acceptance_criteria live in .project/tasks.json; hand-authored templates duplicate them in /tmp/*.md). The duplication is the canon-violation surface — DEC-0019 says scripts/orchestrator/* is the canonical surface and substrate is single-source-of-truth; hand-authored templates contradict both.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add scripts/orchestrator/compile-task-context.ts. Args: --task-id TASK-NNN (reads from .project/tasks.json) + --explore-report <path> (cascade-target table source) + --commit-subject <template>. Output: XML-structured task-block matching the shape compile-implementation-context.ts expects in its --task-template @file arg.

gaps.title: Per-block-kind Nunjucks macros missing for 6 newer block kinds — decisions / spec-reviews / features / framework-gaps / layer-plans / research
gaps.description:
**Per canonical-resolution analysis 2026-05-29 (closure via supersession by TASK-093):** The gap is: **six whole-block delegators missing from `shared/macros.md`** for the newer block kinds, preventing agent templates from rendering contextBlocks-injected whole-block dumps via the canonical `{% from "shared/macros.md" import render_<kind> %}` pattern. Per-item macros exist; the thin wrapper that maps over the block array and delegates to them does not. Each is ~4 lines following the established `render_whole_block_truthy`/`render_whole_block_nonempty` scaffold. Names should follow the file's plural convention: `render_decisions`, `render_features`, `render_framework_gaps`, `render_layer_plans`, `render_research`, `render_spec_reviews` (note: user's original rewrite said `render_all_research`; the implemented name is `render_research` matching the plural convention more directly — implicitly accepted via plan approval).

Original-body corrections (5 disclarities the original FGAP body propagated):
(1) Claim "no per-item macros exist for the 6 kinds" was false — per-item macros existed at `packages/pi-workflows/templates/items/<kind>.md` (14 files); the gap was specifically the absent whole-block *delegator wrappers* in shared/macros.md.
(2) Evidence set was blind to `packages/pi-workflows/templates/items/` — every items/ file existed but went uncited; evidence enumerated only schemas + the symptom + a stale CLAUDE.md note.
(3) Proposed macro names (`render_decision`, `render_feature`, etc. — singular forms) would have *collided* with the existing per-item macros at the same path; the correct convention is plural (`render_decisions`, etc.) per the file's established pattern (`render_tasks`, `render_issues`).
(4) Stale CLAUDE.md note "Per-item macros for newer block kinds ... are pending" was cited as authoritative; CLAUDE.md had drifted from the substrate state.
(5) Impact statement conflated two distinct rendering paths: per-item rendering via `render-item-by-id` (which was unaffected — items/ macros existed) vs whole-block contextBlocks-injected rendering (the actual blocked path).

evidence.reference: single-source of per-block-kind rendering macros; absent macros for the 6 listed kinds

Notes: 'Per-item macros for newer block kinds (decisions, spec-reviews, features, framework-gaps, layer-plans, research) are pending — REVIEW-001 is gated on render_decision.' — implicit gap declaration, not actionable FGAP
REVIEW-001 status not-started; cannot dispatch as JIT agent without render_decision macro
contextBlocks injection implemented; injection works for any block; gap is at the macro layer not the injection layer
block schema defines item shape that the missing render_decision macro would render
schema defines shape for missing render_framework_gaps macro
schema defines shape for missing render_feature macro
schema defines shape for missing render_layer_plan macro
schema defines shape for missing render_research macro
schema defines shape for missing render_spec_review macro

Exhaustive verbatim enumeration of 463 macros-related statements across .project substrate (decisions, framework-gaps, features, tasks, issues, research, verification) + analysis md files + CLAUDE.md; pure enumeration, no synthesis. Reference for canonical scope-grounding when acting on FGAP-037.

Persisted canonical resolution mapping FGAP-037 into the FEAT-001 consumer-cascade arc. Six-element resolution: relocate agent-template tree pi-workflows→pi-jit-agents per DEC-0049 uniform-agent axiom; add 6 missing whole-block delegators with plural-convention names at new canonical location; extend CANONICAL_MACRO_NAMES with array_key column; consumer-cascade the template-search-path; re-file FGAP-037 with corrected body; absorb closure into FEAT-001 as TASK-093.

gaps.impact: Agent templates using `{% from "shared/macros.md" import render_<kind-plural> %}` to render contextBlocks-injected whole-block dumps had no import target for 6 of the newer block kinds (decisions, features, framework-gaps, layer-plans, research, spec-reviews). Per-item rendering via `render-item-by-id` was unaffected; the gap was specifically the absent whole-block delegator wrappers. Closed by relocating the entire agent-template tree to pi-jit-agents (per DEC-0049 uniform-agent axiom) and adding the 6 missing delegators atomically at the canonical location.
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: Nunjucks template macro library — per-record-type rendering primitives
gaps.proposed_resolution: Six whole-block delegators added at `packages/pi-jit-agents/templates/shared/macros.md` (post-relocation canonical location): `render_decisions`, `render_features`, `render_framework_gaps`, `render_layer_plans`, `render_research`, `render_spec_reviews`. Each is a thin wrapper following the established `render_whole_block_truthy` scaffold, importing the per-item macro from `items/<kind>.md` and delegating with `data.<array_key>` honoring the conception.json array-key divergence (framework-gaps→gaps, layer-plans→plans, spec-reviews→reviews). CANONICAL_MACRO_NAMES registry extended to carry the array_key column. Superseded by TASK-093 (filed under FEAT-001) which lands the relocation + delegators + registry extension + consumer rewire atomically. Closing-citation: branch `feat-001-template-relocation`; runtime demo `scripts/orchestrator/runtime-demo-whole-block-delegators.ts`; adversarial probe `/tmp/fgap-037-adversarial-probe.md` (4/4 PASS); persisted canonical-resolution analysis at `analysis/2026-05-29-fgap-037-canonical-resolution.md`.

gaps.title: Missing canonical script: item-level context-block injection for subagent briefs — script doubles as test surface for block-schema shape discovery
gaps.description: scripts/orchestrator/compile-explore-context.ts and compile-implementation-context.ts accept --required-reading <full-file-paths> only — they cannot inject specific substrate items at item-level granularity natively in the composer. Result: Explore agents see full files (or worse, free-form preamble) but not the specific FGAP-035 record, TASK-031 record, DEC-0015 record that constitute the binding context. In pi-runtime the equivalent is FGAP-032 (item-level contextBlocks selectivity in pi-jit-agents). Claude Code orchestrator-side has the same shape of gap with no covering FGAP before this one. Per DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface, both layers need item-level injection. The script doubles as schema-shape discovery test surface: building it forces decisions on selector syntax (block:id vs structured JSON), projection shape (full item / projected fields / macro-rendered), output format (markdown requires macros — FGAP-037; raw JSON; XML-tagged).

evidence.reference: accepts --required-reading file-paths only; no item-level selector arg natively (now integrates inject-context-items.ts via --context-items)
same constraint; --context-items integration landed

this turn first Explore dispatch surfaced OR-hedging in cascade-target Required-change cells because the context omitted TASK-031 UX-decision (lived in ~/.claude/plans/*.md, not substrate); direct evidence of the missing-injection failure mode
FGAP-032 captures pi-runtime side (item-level contextBlocks); FGAP-038 is the Claude Code-side counterpart
DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface — both consumer layers need item-level injection
DEC-0014 — substrate IS operating surface; orchestrator-side scripts that cannot inject substrate items at granularity break the dogfooding goal

gaps.impact: Subagent briefs that need specific FGAP/TASK/DEC items injected as binding context currently route via free-form question text or full-file --required-reading. Free-form text propagates orchestrator-side rephrasing (LLM-summarizes-then-summarizes-again drift); full-file reading bloats agent context window with irrelevant content. Both failure modes surface in /tmp/explore-fgap035-canonical.md's row 5+7 OR-hedging — the agent didn't know about TASK-031's resolved UX decision because no clean injection path delivered it. Without the script, every subagent dispatch this turn has gone out under-contextualized; the canonical surface degradation falls back on hand-authored briefs (FGAP-036 root cause). The block-schema shape discovery angle compounds the impact: not having the script means schemas evolve without the constraint-driven-design that item-level injection would impose.
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: item-level context block injection — work-unit-driven substrate projection into agent prompts
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add scripts/orchestrator/inject-context-items.ts. Args: --items <block:itemId,...> selector list + --format <markdown|json|xml> output mode + optional --fields <fieldList> projection. Output: structured context-block content for inclusion in compile-explore-context.ts / compile-implementation-context.ts via a new --context-items arg, or piped directly into Agent prompt. Per FGAP-037 dependency: markdown format requires per-block-kind render macros.

gaps.title: compile-*-context.ts composers carry hardcoded investigation-specific content in multiple sections — over-fitted to FGAP-026 fixture-cascade audit shape
gaps.description: scripts/orchestrator/compile-explore-context.ts AND compile-implementation-context.ts both embed investigation-specific content in hardcoded string literals across multiple XML sections, contradicting the structural-shell intent. When the composers are reused for a different investigation (e.g. FGAP-035 cascade-target inventory), the hardcoded sections contradict the per-investigation question. Root pattern: any investigation-specific content baked into a structural composer is the same bug shape, regardless of which section it sits in.

gaps.impact: Composers over-fit to first use case; reuse for new investigations propagates contradictory instructions into the agent context. gaps.proposed_resolution: Refactor BOTH composers to pure structural shells; every investigation-specific section becomes a --*-path arg loading a markdown fragment from scripts/orchestrator/templates/<composer>-<section>-<shape>.md. gaps.title: tasks[].phase carries free-text sub-phase labels (e.g. "8.7.1") instead of FK references to PHASE-NNN phase block items
gaps.description: .project/tasks.json items use task.phase as a free-text string holding sub-phase labels from the canonical FGAP-026 sub-phase decomposition (e.g. TASK-031/032/033 carry phase: "8.7.1"). The substrate also defines a phase block kind (PHASE-NNN id pattern via packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/phase.schema.json) whose items would be the canonical FK target. Currently phase block items for the FGAP-026 closure plan are not populated — HANDOFF documents PHASE-006..010 as scheduled for Phase 5 of the FGAP-026 closure plan (the arc-tracking-blocks dogfood deliverable per TASK-025). Until Phase 5 lands the PHASE-006..010 substrate, every task.phase field carrying a sub-phase label is a free-text placeholder rather than a typed reference. Per DEC-0013 (closure-table is canonical primitive for ALL inter-item relationships), the proper representation is either (a) PHASE-NNN id as FK string OR (b) closure-table edge with relation_type = belongs_to_phase. Free-text labels are neither.

evidence.reference: TASK-031/032/033 carry phase: "8.7.1" — free-text sub-phase label, not a PHASE-NNN FK; same shape for TASK-021..030 carrying phase: "8.7.1" through "8.7.10"
Phase block schema exists; PHASE-NNN id pattern; ready to receive the FGAP-026 closure plan phase items
HANDOFF documents PHASE-006..010 as scheduled for Phase 5 of FGAP-026 closure (TASK-025 arc-tracking-blocks dogfood deliverable)
DEC-0013 closure-table is canonical primitive for ALL inter-item relationships — free-text phase labels violate this

gaps.impact: DEC-0013 canon violation: tasks[].phase carries free-text labels where the canon mandates closure-table edges for inter-item relationships. Validators surfacing the gap correctly flag tasks[].phase as unresolvable references; cross-block phase queries (e.g. all tasks in PHASE-007) cannot run; status-by-phase rollup (per roadmap-plan PM-lens semantics) misses these tasks until phase substrate is populated. Every task added during the FGAP-026 closure carries the same free-text shape; migration scope grows with each filing.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Resolved by Phase 5 (TASK-025) execution: ship PHASE-006..010 phase items + relations.json edges (task → phase via relation_type = belongs_to_phase per DEC-0013 closure-table-only convention) + migration via schema-migrations.ts rewriting existing task.phase free-text labels into typed relations.json edges. tasks schema deprecates the inline phase field (or removes it) once relations.json carries the references. Substantive sub-gap of FGAP-026 phase 5 — fix vehicle is TASK-025 execution, not a parallel arc.

gaps.title: issues[].resolved_by carries free-text (commit SHAs / commit messages) instead of VER-NNN verification-entry references — substrate-canon evidence layer not adopted for 26 existing resolved issues
gaps.description: .project/issues.json carries 26 resolved issues whose resolved_by field contains free-text (commit SHAs, commit messages, plain-string author identifiers) rather than VER-NNN ids referencing entries in the verification block. Pre-this-turn convention treated resolved_by as a free-text annotation; this turn established the verification block kind (.project/verification.json) as the canonical evidence layer with VER-NNN ids carrying structured fields (target, target_type, status, method, evidence, criteria_results[]). For substrate-canon consistency, resolved_by should reference a VER-NNN id (FK string OR closure-table edge with relation_type = verified_by) so resolution evidence lives in the typed verification block rather than as inline free-text fields on the issue.

evidence.reference: 26 issues with status=resolved carry resolved_by free-text values (commit SHAs / commit messages / author identifiers); inconsistent shape across issues
VER-001/002/003/004 filed this turn establish the verification block kind as canonical evidence layer with structured criteria_results[] per VER
resolved_by field shape: free-text string; no FK constraint against VER-NNN

VER schema requires id, target, target_type, status, method; verification.target_type enum doesn't currently include 'issue' — issues schema would need to enumerate FK target OR verification.target_type would need to extend
DEC-0013 closure-table is canonical for inter-item relationships; FK-as-field is parallel ungated path

gaps.impact: DEC-0013 canon violation across 26 existing resolved issues + every future issue-resolution. Inter-item references (issue → verification) live as free-text fields rather than closure-table edges. Cross-cutting: same pattern in tasks[].verification (added this turn as FK-as-field — also a DEC-0013 violation that must be migrated). Decisions[].enacted_by + framework-gaps[].closed_by reference IDENTITIES not block items so DEC-0013 governs them only if/when identities become substrate-block items (separate substrate-canon question).
gaps.proposed_resolution: Per DEC-0013 (closure-table is canonical for ALL inter-item relationships): (1) Extend verification.schema.json target_type enum to include issue + task + decision + framework-gap so all four block kinds become legal verification targets. (2) Add relation_type = verified_by to config.relation_types[] registry. (3) Migrate the 26 existing resolved issues via schema-migrations.ts: for each issue with free-text resolved_by, author a retrospective VER-NNN capturing the closure evidence + write a relations.json edge (issue-X --verified_by--> VER-Y). (4) Migrate the FK-as-field violations introduced this turn (tasks[].verification carrying VER-NNN as inline string field for TASK-021/031/032/033) into closure-table edges of the same shape — accepts mandate-007 correction of substrate-canon violation introduced during VER-001..004 filing. (5) Deprecate / remove issues[].resolved_by + tasks[].verification inline string fields once edges carry the references. Out of scope: decisions[].enacted_by + framework-gaps[].closed_by carry identity strings (human/agent author refs), not block-item refs — DEC-0013 applies only when identities themselves become substrate-block items.

gaps.title: Precise-diagnostic-without-repair-surface pattern: validators surface unresolvable diagnostics with no repair tool — UX pattern unfiled
gaps.description: Multiple pi-context validator tools emit precise actionable diagnostics that the harness-confined main LLM (DEC-0014) cannot resolve through any registered tool. Surfaced by constrained-harness LLM evaluation under --tools pi-context-only constraint. Specific instances: project-validate-relations returns 'No .project/config.json — cannot validate relations'; project-edges-for-lens returns 'No .project/config.json — run /project init first'; project-validate returns 'tasks[TASK-031].phase references 8.7.1 which does not exist' (×10). None of these conditions has a repair tool — config.json authoring is Phase 5 (TASK-025) substrate-canon work; tasks[].phase free-text labels are FGAP-040 territory. Pattern: substrate exposes diagnostic surface precisely but the repair surface is absent. FGAP-028 names 'subcommand-only surfaces unreachable under DEC-0014' adjacent but doesn't capture the precise-diagnostic-orphan-repair pattern itself.

evidence.reference: project-validate-relations + project-edges-for-lens + project-validate tool error messages name conditions that have no repair-tool counterpart in the registered tool surface
FGAP-040 (tasks[].phase free-text) + FGAP-041 (issues[].resolved_by free-text) describe substrate violations whose validator-flagged-but-unrepairable instances surface as this pattern
DEC-0014 (harness-confined main LLM) — diagnostics that can only be repaired by exiting the harness violate the substrate-IS-operating-surface principle

gaps.impact: Under DEC-0014 a harness-confined main LLM receiving an unresolvable diagnostic has no path forward via the tool surface. The diagnostic surface is precise; the repair surface is absent — substrate-canon-wise the validator surface should pair with repair tools OR the diagnostic should defer with a tool-routable next-step pointer. Live: substrate writes outside the tool surface (e.g. Edit on .project/*.json) are forbidden per F-006 / DEC-0016; so the harness-confined LLM legitimately has no path. Compounds with FGAP-028 (subcommand-only surfaces) as the substrate-surface UX gap.
gaps.proposed_resolution: (1) Audit all validator / read tools in pi-context that emit error strings naming substrate states the LLM cannot resolve via the tool surface. (2) For each: either pair with a repair tool (e.g. config-init writes a minimal config.json from prompts; task-update-phase rewrites a free-text phase value; relations-add adds a closure-table edge) OR change the diagnostic to point at the canonical repair path that DOES exist (e.g. error includes 'use context-init Phase 6 tool when shipped' OR 'this is Phase 5 substrate-canon work tracked at TASK-025'). (3) Substrate-canon principle: every validator-emitted error string SHOULD identify either a repair tool, an in-progress task, or a known-deferred phase — never a dead-end. Implementation arc: tied to Phase 4 + Phase 5 + Phase 6 of FGAP-026 closure (workflow-execute tools + arc-tracking-blocks + context-init/install/migrate respectively); FGAP-042 closes when validators have repair-tool pairs OR explicit deferred-state pointers.

gaps.title: Composite/join operations across blocks missing at tool surface — N+1 read pattern for natural queries (e.g. tasks × verifications join)
gaps.description: Natural cross-block queries require multiple round-trip tool calls. Example: 'show me all completed tasks with their verifications' requires read-block(tasks) → filter-block-items(tasks, status=completed) → for each completed task: resolve-item-by-id(verification VER-id). Minimum 3 calls (or N+1 if resolved per item) for a single natural query. The closure-table primitives (walkDescendants / walkAncestors / findReferences) traverse edges but don't compose with cross-block resolution at the tool surface; gatherExecutionContext (TASK-039) is unit-driven for the DEC-0017 context-bundle use-case, not a general cross-block join. Surfaced by constrained-harness LLM evaluation: 'show completed tasks with their verifications' couldn't be answered in one tool call.

evidence.reference: Registered tool surface: filter-block-items + resolve-item-by-id + resolve-items-by-id are independent primitives; no compose-join tool wraps them for FK-style block-to-block joins
gatherExecutionContext composes per unit-kind context-contract; not a general join primitive for arbitrary block pairs
DEC-0014 — harness-confined main LLM bears the cost of N round-trip calls when one composite call would suffice

gaps.impact: N+1 round-trip cost on natural cross-block queries when the substrate has the data to satisfy them in one composite call. Under DEC-0014 each tool call is a context-window event; N+1 calls multiply prompt + response tokens proportionally. Substrate is queryable; the tool surface forces decomposition the substrate doesn't require.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add a join-blocks-on-field tool: signature `joinBlocks(cwd, leftBlock: string, rightBlock: string, leftField: string, rightField: string, leftPredicate?: FilterPredicate): Array<{left: ItemRecord, right: ItemRecord | null}>` returning paired records where leftItem[leftField] === rightItem[rightField]. Library function in project-sdk.ts; pi tool + orchestrator script per DEC-0019 dual-surface. Optional leftPredicate parameter filters the left side before join (covers the 'completed tasks × verifications' case via leftPredicate={field:status, op:eq, value:completed}). Out of scope: many-to-many joins (require composite key shape; addressable later via closure-table walks once relations.json has real edges per Phase 5).

gaps.title: No tool discovery mechanism for harness-confined main LLM — list-tools / tool-introspection tool unfiled
gaps.description:
Under DEC-0014 the harness-confined main LLM operates only through extension-registered tools. There is no registered tool that lists the available tools, their parameters, descriptions, or surface coverage. Tool discovery happens only via context-window injection (the orchestrator hand-curates which tool names the LLM sees) OR via grep on packages/pi-context/src/index.ts — neither path is available to the harness-confined LLM via its tool surface. As the registered tool count grows (currently 28 in pi-context alone) tool-name discovery via guessing becomes the de facto mechanism. Surfaced by constrained-harness LLM evaluation: 'I know what tools exist because my context says so. An LLM without that context has no list-tools or help surface.'

SDK-NATIVE PRIMITIVE (2026-05-21, Pi SDK investigation — analysis/2026-05-21-pi-sdk-0.74-to-0.75-investigation.md): the Pi SDK ALREADY exposes tool enumeration on ExtensionAPI — pi.getAllTools(): ToolInfo[] (ToolInfo = Pick<ToolDefinition, name|description|parameters> + extras) and pi.getActiveTools(): string[]. Present in BOTH 0.74.0 (current) and 0.75.4 — version-independent, available NOW (does NOT gate on the SDK upgrade), reachable from a tool execute via closure over the extension-factory pi. This gap shrinks to WRAPPING pi.getAllTools(), not reinventing enumeration.

evidence.reference: 28 pi.registerTool({...}) calls; no list-tools / tools-status / introspect-tools tool registered
DEC-0014 — substrate IS operating surface; tool discovery should itself be a tool, not a side-channel

gaps.impact: Under DEC-0014 a fresh harness-confined LLM session cannot enumerate its own capabilities. The orchestrator must inject the tool list via context. This contradicts the substrate-IS-operating-surface principle: the LLM's view of available capabilities lives outside the substrate / outside the tool surface. As tools grow the gap compounds.
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: tool surface introspection / tool registry as queryable substrate
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add a list-tools (tools-status) pi tool that WRAPS the SDK-native enumeration pi.getAllTools(): ToolInfo[] (name/description/parameters) + pi.getActiveTools(): string[] — both on ExtensionAPI, available in 0.74.0+, reachable from execute via closure over the extension-factory pi. Do NOT scan packages/*/src/index.ts or read registerTool internal state — the SDK provides the primitive (corrected 2026-05-21 from the original scan/internal-state approach). If label/promptSnippet/package are wanted beyond ToolInfo, supplement from our own registration metadata. Pi tool wrapper + orchestrator CLI per DEC-0019 dual-surface. Companion: author every tool description/promptSnippet as discovery-ready (terse, action-shaped) since ToolInfo surfaces description.

gaps.title: No item-level read at tool surface — read-block is all-or-nothing; no read-block-item / paginated read; large blocks force whole-file fetch
gaps.description: read-block returns the entire block's JSON. Large blocks (decisions ~109KB, framework-gaps growing similarly) force whole-file fetch even when the caller wants one item. resolve-item-by-id covers by-ID lookup; filter-block-items covers predicate filter; but there's no read-block-item(block, id) for direct item fetch by ID without the block roundtrip, and no paginated read for blocks too large to inject. Distinct from FGAP-014 (pagination in renderers like renderLensView / renderRoadmap) and FGAP-032 (item-level INJECTION at JIT-agent dispatch via contextBlocks selectivity). This is item-level READ at the tool API surface for harness-confined main LLM consumption.

evidence.reference: read-block tool returns whole block via readBlock from block-api; no item-by-id variant + no paginated variant
FGAP-014 (renderer pagination) + FGAP-032 (item-level contextBlocks injection at dispatch) are adjacent but distinct — neither covers tool-API item-level read
DEC-0014 — context-window economy under harness confinement makes whole-block-fetch-when-one-item-wanted a substrate-surface inefficiency

gaps.impact: Context-window cost when caller wants one item from a large block. read-block-item via filter-block-items + take(1) requires two tool calls (filter then implicit pick); resolve-item-by-id covers when the ID is known but not when discovery + read should compose. No paginated read means large blocks can exceed the truncateHead 50KB threshold (already observed; truncation message surfaces). Under growing substrate this gap compounds.
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: item-level read at tool surface; paginated block-read
gaps.proposed_resolution: (1) Add read-block-item(cwd, block, id) tool: equivalent to filter-block-items(block, id, eq) + take(1) but as a single primitive returning the item OR null. (2) Add read-block-page(cwd, block, offset, limit) for paginated reads of large blocks; returns `{items: ItemRecord[], total: number, hasMore: boolean}`. (3) read-block stays for full-block fetches; truncateHead behavior unchanged. Library + pi tool + orchestrator script per DEC-0019 dual-surface.

gaps.title: tasks[].depends_on inline FK array — third instance of FGAP-040/041 DEC-0013 violation pattern; substrate-wide audit needed
gaps.description: tasks schema declares depends_on: array<string> on each task item; TASK-040 carries depends_on: ['TASK-039']; multiple tasks across .project/tasks.json carry such inline FK arrays. Per DEC-0013 closure-table-only-for-inter-item-relationships, depends_on inline arrays are forbidden — references must live as relations.json edges with relation_type=depends_on. Same root pattern as FGAP-040 (tasks[].phase free-text label) + FGAP-041 (issues[].resolved_by free-text). Third instance triggers a substrate-wide audit obligation: every existing schema's array<string> field that references other block items is a DEC-0013 candidate violation. Surfaced by constrained-harness LLM evaluation.

evidence.reference: tasks[].depends_on declared as array<string> — inline FK array shape
TASK-040 + others carry depends_on values referencing other TASK-NNN items as inline strings rather than closure-table edges
FGAP-040 + FGAP-041 same root pattern; FGAP-046 is the third explicit instance
DEC-0013 — closure-table is canonical primitive for ALL inter-item relationships; FK-as-field forbidden; no carve-out for depends_on

gaps.impact: Substrate-wide DEC-0013 violation across tasks.json (multiple items carrying depends_on inline) and potentially other schemas. Cross-cutting pattern observation: the substrate audit must enumerate every array<string> field on every schema and classify each as (a) genuine inline data field (not an inter-item reference) OR (b) DEC-0013-violating FK array requiring migration to closure-table. Per pattern in FGAP-040/041 the migration vehicle is Phase 5 (TASK-025 / arc-tracking-blocks dogfood + schema-migrations.ts) — the same migration arc that lands relations.json + config.relation_types[].
gaps.proposed_resolution: (1) Substrate-wide audit of every registry schema's array<string> fields; classify each as data-field OR FK-array. (2) For each FK-array: migration to relations.json closure-table edges with declared relation_type (depends_on / related / etc.) registered in config.relation_types[]. (3) Schema modifications via schema-migrations.ts version bumps removing the inline array. (4) Existing block items: per-item migration writing the inline values as relations.json edges + removing the inline field. (5) Migration scope tied to Phase 5 + 6 of FGAP-026 closure; FGAP-040/041/046 + future-discovered instances share the same migration arc — file each surfaced instance against this canonical pattern; do not relitigate per-field.

gaps.title: Vocabulary settlement upstream of Phase 5 LLM-filed decomposition skipped — display layer, ID conventions, relation_type names, lens ids, layer registry, status-bucket mapping unsettled
gaps.description: The LLM-filed Phase 5 sub-phase decomposition (analysis/2026-05-10-fgap-026-closure-sub-phase-structure.md line 14: 5.1 config / 5.2 relations / 5.3 roadmap / 5.4 phase / 5.5 context-contracts / 5.6 tasks-phase-edges) assumed vocabulary was settled but no upstream settlement step was filed. Concretely unsettled: (1) display layer — block-kind display_name + per-item ID-prefix display rendering have no canonical settlement; (2) ID prefix conventions — padding (3-digit vs 4-digit), casing (uppercase vs lowercase) have observed drift (see FGAP-048); (3) relation_type canonical_ids — DEC-0013 forced relation_type registry but no relation_types are declared anywhere except code test fixtures (phase_depends_on / phase_member); (4) lens ids — config.lenses[] would carry projection definitions but no lens-id naming convention exists; (5) layer registry — L1..L5 referenced in FGAP layer field but no .project/schemas/layer.schema.json or config.layers[] populated (FGAP-016 was closed but the registry remains unauthored); (6) status-bucket mapping — FGAP-021 tracks the 4 divergent lifecycle vocabularies (workflow-state / gate-state / authority-state / authoring-state) reconciliation but no status_buckets[] declared. Result: Phase 5 sub-phase 5.1 (config.json authoring) cannot proceed coherently — every block_kinds[] / relation_types[] / lenses[] / layers[] / status_buckets[] entry needs a settled convention before being authored.

evidence.reference: 7 open decisions enumerated; vocabulary is upstream of decisions 1+4+7
Reverse-engineered config draft surfaces L1+L2 inventory + drift + missing-schemas
Patterns derivable from existing substrate for L3+L4+L5+L6
Cross-doc mapping of decisions to analysis content
Exhaustive PM-vocabulary external-survey analysis
Line 14: Phase 5 sub-phases enumeration assuming vocabulary settled

gaps.impact: Phase 5 (TASK-025) cannot complete acceptance criterion 1 ("config.json declares relation_types[]...") without upstream vocabulary settlement. Every config.block_kinds[].display_name + relation_type canonical_id + lens id + layer canonical_id needs a settled convention; without them, authoring is improvised per session and accumulates drift. Downstream: relations.json edge writes (DEC-0013 closure-table) need relation_type names settled; roadmap.json phases[].lens references need lens-id naming settled; status rollups across blocks need status_buckets[] settled. Sub-phases 5.2 / 5.3 / 5.5 / 5.6 all gate on this work.
gaps.canonical_vocabulary: config.block_kinds[].display_name, config.relation_types[].canonical_id, config.relation_types[].display_name, config.lenses[].id, config.layers[].id, config.status_buckets
gaps.proposed_resolution: Open DEC entries per unsettled convention; user direction enacts each; settled DECs cascade into convention rules (conventions[].rules[]) + config.json content. Specific DEC questions: (a) prefix-padding convention (3-digit canonical; R- and DEC- 4-digit are drift); (b) prefix-casing convention (uppercase canonical; issue- is drift); (c) PHASE-NNN ↔ phase.json `number` integer binding; (d) substrate-dir replacement name (FGAP-026 deliverable); (e) layer registry adoption (L1 substrate / L2 query / L3 composition / L4 dispatch / L5 surface labels per analysis/gsd-2-foundational-intelligence.md); (f) status-bucket normalization mapping per FGAP-021; (g) relation_type naming shape (`<source>_<verb>_<target>` per existing test fixtures). TASK-042 scopes the settlement work-unit; each DEC enacts atomically at user direction.

gaps.title: ID-prefix convention drift across existing block kinds — issue- lowercase + R-/DEC- 4-digit vs all others 3-digit uppercase
gaps.description: Inspection of current .project/*.json block instances (analysis/2026-05-14-step-1-config-draft-from-substrate.md drift section) surfaces two specific prefix-convention drifts: (1) casing — `issue-001`/`issue-002`/etc. use lowercase prefix while DEC- / FGAP- / TASK- / VER- / FEAT- / PLAN- / REVIEW- / CTX- / RAT- all use uppercase; (2) padding — `R-0001` (research) + `DEC-0001..0023` (decisions) use 4-digit zero-padding while FGAP- (3-digit) / TASK- (3-digit) / VER- (3-digit) / FEAT- (3-digit) / PLAN- (3-digit) / REVIEW- (3-digit) / CTX- (3-digit) / RAT- (3-digit) all use 3-digit. Drift accumulated as block kinds were authored at different times by different sessions without canonical convention settlement (FGAP-047 root cause).

evidence.reference: All issue IDs use 'issue-' lowercase prefix; 91 items
All research IDs use 'R-NNNN' 4-digit zero-pad; 11 items
All DEC IDs use 'DEC-NNNN' 4-digit zero-pad; 23 items now
All FGAP IDs use 'FGAP-NNN' 3-digit zero-pad; uppercase; 47 items now
Drift surfaced in convention-drift section of config draft

gaps.impact: ID-prefix lens binning + cross-block ID parsing assumes uniform shape; current drift forces per-prefix branching at the lens-bin layer + complicates ID-pattern regex composition. LLM lookups grep across blocks assume one prefix shape per block. Display rendering needs to handle 3 distinct shapes (lowercase / 3-digit / 4-digit). Forced consistency post-hoc requires migration of existing IDs which cascades to every relations.json edge that ever references them.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Settle two DECs: (a) prefix-padding convention — 3-digit canonical; R- and DEC- migrate forward to 3-digit (preserving leading zero significance — DEC-0001 → DEC-001 would collide at ID-9 boundary, so likely retain 4-digit for high-count blocks OR introduce wider canonical width). Alternative: declare 4-digit canonical and migrate FGAP- TASK- etc. (b) prefix-casing — uppercase canonical; issue- migrates to ISSUE-. Migration impact: every relations.json edge + every analysis MD + every commit message referencing migrated IDs needs updating. Practical preservation: declare canonical convention going forward; existing drift items remain in current shape with a 'historical-convention' note OR are batch-migrated in a single arc-completion sweep.

gaps.title: Phase block-kind pattern divergence — file-per-phase at phases/{number}.json with integer discriminator vs array-block convention used by all other block kinds
gaps.description: Every other block kind in .project/ uses the array-block pattern: one JSON file per block kind (e.g. decisions.json), top-level array_key (e.g. 'decisions'), items with string id field matching a canonical prefix pattern. Phase block-kind diverges: schema (phase.schema.json) requires integer 'number' field as discriminator; file path is phases/{number}.json (one file per phase, not one file per block kind); no top-level array. Roadmap block (roadmap.schema.json) references phases via string 'id' matching ^PHASE-[A-Z0-9-]+$ pattern — different from phase.schema.json's integer discriminator. The binding convention (PHASE-NNN string ↔ integer number) is declared nowhere. Result: phase items don't appear in standard block-item enumeration; can't be filed via file-block-item.ts append surface (which expects array-block shape); resolve-items-by-id won't find PHASE-NNN unless special-cased.

evidence.reference: Requires 'number: integer' as primary discriminator; no id field
phases[] items require 'id' matching ^PHASE-[A-Z0-9-]+$ string pattern
Directory contains {1,2,3,4}.json (pre-FGAP-026 planning artifacts) using integer discriminator
Tests use PHASE-A / PHASE-B / PHASE-C single-letter suffix; LLM-filed commit body f3d6e41 uses PHASE-006..010 numeric suffix; no canonical convention
Phase row in block_kinds[] flagged with 'file-per-phase, no array_key' anomaly

gaps.impact: TASK-025 sub-phase 5.4 (phase.json authoring) cannot proceed without resolving the binding convention. resolve-items-by-id / filter-block-items / walk-ancestors all assume array-block shape with string-id items; phase items invisible to those primitives without special handling. Lens projections referencing PHASE-NNN as bins don't connect to phase block-kind file storage without a binding rule. Pre-existing phases/{1..4}.json files occupy integer slots 1..4, blocking number=1..4 reuse without prior archive/repurpose decision.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Settle DEC binding integer number ↔ PHASE-NNN string id (e.g. PHASE-${number:03d}). Either: (a) extend phase.schema.json to include both fields with binding constraint; OR (b) migrate phase block to array-block pattern (single phases.json file with phases[] array, items with id field) which aligns with all other block kinds + enables canonical query primitives. Option (b) is more aligned with substrate canon (DEC-0013 + lens infrastructure) but requires migrating existing phases/{1..4}.json content. Cross-decision with pre-FGAP-026 phases disposition (which is in FGAP-047 settlement scope).

gaps.title: Framework-shaped schemas (config / relations / roadmap / bootstrap) authored in pi-context/registry/schemas/ but not installed in .project/schemas/
gaps.description: pi-context/registry/schemas/ ships 25 schemas including framework-shaped ones (config.schema.json / relations.schema.json / roadmap.schema.json / bootstrap.schema.json / layer.schema.json / plan.schema.json / priority.schema.json / severity.schema.json / source.schema.json / status.schema.json / verification-method.schema.json). .project/schemas/ holds 23 schemas covering user-block kinds (decisions / framework-gaps / tasks / etc.) but is missing the framework-shaped 4: config / relations / roadmap / bootstrap. Without config.schema.json in .project/schemas/, config.json instance cannot be AJV-validated via the installed-schema resolution path. Without relations.schema.json, relations.json edges cannot be schema-validated. Without roadmap.schema.json, roadmap.json instance authoring blocked. Bootstrap pointer is the .pi-context.json file at repo root; bootstrap.schema.json validates that pointer — currently this repo's .pi-context.json exists but is not AJV-validated against an installed schema.

evidence.reference: 25 schema files including config.schema.json + relations.schema.json + roadmap.schema.json + bootstrap.schema.json
23 schema files; missing config / relations / roadmap / bootstrap
Schema declares root, naming, layers, block_kinds, status_buckets, display_strings, relation_types, hierarchy, lenses, installed_schemas, installed_blocks
Schema validates closure-table edges (parent, child, relation_type, ordinal)

gaps.impact: TASK-025 sub-phases 5.1 (config.json) + 5.2 (relations.json) + 5.3 (roadmap.json) cannot author instances against AJV until schemas installed. Phase 5 sub-phase 5.1 implicitly requires this schema-install step. Current substrate canon cascades (block-api appendToBlock validates against schemas via resolver) silently treat absent schemas as 'no schema available' — falling back to no validation, which violates DEC-0013 + DEC-0017 invariants for the framework-shaped blocks.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Install the 4 framework-shaped schemas to .project/schemas/ as part of Phase 5 prep (or as a discrete sub-step in TASK-025 / TASK-042). Pattern: cp packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/{config,relations,roadmap,bootstrap}.schema.json .project/schemas/. After install, authoring config.json + relations.json + roadmap.json via file-block-item.ts or canonical block-api routes will AJV-validate. Cross-decision with FGAP-051 (other registry-shipped schemas) for whether to install the supporting enum-shaped schemas (priority / severity / source / status / layer / plan / verification-method) — those are referenced via $ref from user-block schemas but don't require their own data instance. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 — DEC-0043 (1d5ee89) + commit 752e7e2: framework schemas (config/relations/roadmap/bootstrap) are bundled-validated, NOT installable; installed_schemas = block-kinds only. The install-the-framework-schemas premise is superseded by bundled validation (loadConfig/loadRelations use bundledSchemaPath). config + relations dropped from the conception installed_schemas.]

gaps.title: Conventions block schema underspecified for relational / lens / lookup discoverability — flat slug-id rules with no category / applies_to / related_decisions / title / lifecycle / source
gaps.description: Conventions block (.project/schemas/conventions.schema.json + .project/conventions.json) was authored pre-DEC-0013 + pre-lens-infrastructure. Schema item shape: id (freeform slug like 'esm' / 'tsc-build' / 'no-pi-dir'), description (single payload), enforcement (lint/test/review/manual), severity (error/warning/info). Missing facets that the surrounding lens / relational / lookup canon requires: (1) no category / domain field — LLMs cannot lens 'filing conventions' vs 'naming conventions' vs 'commit conventions' vs 'build conventions'; 16 entries are flat-list. (2) No applies_to field — no surface scoping (substrate / source / tests / commit / specific-package). (3) No related_decisions — conventions emerging from DECs have no closure-table edge home; relations.json cannot carry the linkage canonically. (4) No title field separate from description — listings cannot render compactly; no scan-friendly facet for filter-block-items. (5) Id is freeform slug, no canonical prefix pattern — cannot pattern-match by lens bin shape; cross-block id resolution inconsistent. (6) No status / lifecycle — conventions accumulate without supersession or retirement tracking. (7) No source / citation field — conventions don't cite the file or DEC that enforces them. (8) No canonical_vocabulary field (cf. FGAP schema). Same shape question applies to other zero-item or early-authored blocks: architecture, domain, requirements — those schemas may equally predate the relational layer + lens infrastructure that DEC-0013 + lens-view.ts + project-context.ts established.

evidence.reference: Item shape: id / description / enforcement / severity — no category / applies_to / related_decisions / title / status / source / canonical_vocabulary
16 flat-list rules; first 3: 'esm' / 'tsc-build' / 'no-pi-dir' — slug ids, no facets

Comparison: FGAP has title / status / priority / package / layer / canonical_vocabulary / impact / evidence / proposed_resolution / related_features / related_decisions / related_issues — facet-rich shape
Comparison: DEC has title / status / context / decision / consequences / supersedes / superseded_by / related_findings / related_features / related_gaps / references — also facet-rich
Lens projection assumes items have facets for bin grouping
filterBlockItems / walkAncestors / findReferences operate on facet-bearing items

gaps.impact: LLM looking up 'how to file substrate' or 'what build commands to use' must grep description strings — no facet supports the query. filter-block-items predicate cannot bin conventions by domain. walk-ancestors cannot traverse from a DEC to its enacted convention rule (no edge field). Cross-block discoverability of conventions is degraded compared to facet-rich blocks. The same underspecification likely affects architecture / domain / requirements schemas (all zero-item; authored pre-relational-layer). Affects any LLM trying to enter project context via conventions-block as a query entry point.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Schema-migrate conventions.schema.json to add: title (scan-friendly summary), category (substrate-filing / naming / commit / build / monitoring / etc.), applies_to (target surface), status (active / retired / superseded), related_decisions (closure-table edge home via relations.json), source (file path or DEC citation), canonical_vocabulary (engineering terminology). Migrate existing 16 rules to populate new fields. Similar audit + migration for architecture / domain / requirements / project schemas — surface their relational/lens/lookup gaps via this FGAP's resolution sweep. Cross-decision with FGAP-047 vocabulary settlement (display-name + categorization) and FGAP-048 id-prefix drift (canonical prefix CONV- vs continued freeform slug).

gaps.title: config.layers[] + block_kinds[].layer FK + lens-validator dispatch are declarable in config but unconsumed at runtime — config-driven-from-line-1 (DEC-0015) partially delivered
gaps.description: Grep audit of packages/pi-context/src/ surfaces three runtime-vs-declaration gaps: (1) config.layers[] — declared in packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/config.schema.json (work-altitude tier registry with id + display_name + description) — has ZERO consuming code paths. loadConfig reads it (project-context.ts:175,188,274) but no downstream usage of cfg.layers; filter-block-items / walkAncestors / lens-view / roadmap-plan never read it. Declaration is symbolic only. (2) config.block_kinds[].layer FK — schema permits optional layer reference per block-kind; no code groups, filters, projects, or rolls up by this FK. (3) Lens-validator dispatch — registerLensValidator() in lens-view.ts is code-registered (module-init); user-added lens kinds require code, not just config.lenses[] declaration. Result: framework intention per DEC-0015 ("config drives substrate location; config is the source of truth from line 1") is structurally undelivered for the work-altitude + lens-validator surfaces. A user can declare a custom layer scheme (e.g. initiative / epic / story / task / subtask) in config.layers[] and assign block_kinds[].layer references, but nothing in pi-context's runtime will honor those declarations — query primitives don't filter by altitude, lens projections don't bin by altitude, roadmap rollups don't group by altitude. [2026-05-20 refinement (DEC-0039 no-optional): the layers degenerate default is 1 IMPLICIT layer — when config.layers is empty, all block_kinds belong to a single implicit layer and block_kinds[].layer resolves to it. This is the concrete semantic layers consumption must implement, and it distinguishes layers (the one legitimate degenerate default) from the other registries (empty = genuine none).]

evidence.reference: Lines 25-38: layers[] schema declaration; lines 39-55: block_kinds[].layer FK declared
Lines 175, 188, 274: loadConfig reads config but no consumer reads .layers[] downstream
registerLensValidator pattern — code-side module-init registration; not config-driven
groupByLens projects via lens.bins; bins are arbitrary strings, not driven by config.layers[] altitude tiers
FGAP entries use 'layer: L1/L2/L3/L4' free-text field; no validation against any installed layer registry

gaps.impact: DEC-0015 substrate-canon claim is partially delivered. User attempting to set up custom work-altitude scheme (e.g. roadmap → milestone → epic → story → task → subtask) purely through config + schemas + macros would find that declarations parse and AJV-validate but produce no runtime altitude-aware behavior. Cross-package consumers (pi-workflows / pi-behavior-monitors) cannot rely on config.layers[] for any decision-making. The framework intention/reality gap is not currently visible as substrate. Phase 5 sub-phase 5.1 (config.json authoring) would surface this if config.layers[] is populated — the entries would have no consuming behavior. Cross-decision with FGAP-016 (closed) which surfaced similar declaration-without-consumer pattern for enum vocabularies.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Three sub-resolutions: (a) Wire config.layers[] consumption into filter-block-items / walkAncestors / lens-view as a layer-aware predicate option; document which primitives honor layer filtering. (b) Add layer-aware projection helper in roadmap-plan that groups items by block_kinds[].layer FK + emits altitude-stratified roadmap rollups. (c) Move lens-validator dispatch from code-side registerLensValidator to config-side — lens-kind specs declared in config.lenses[] include a validator-strategy enum referencing built-in strategies; user-added strategies still need code but standard ones cascade from config. Acceptance: user can declare 5-tier altitude scheme in config.layers[], assign all block_kinds[].layer references, run filter-block-items + walkAncestors + roadmap rollup, and observe altitude-honored behavior without code changes. Cross-decision with FGAP-047 (vocabulary settlement) and FGAP-051 (conventions schema) — same underlying principle that pi-context's substrate canon must runtime-honor what config declares.

gaps.title: Milestone is inline within roadmap.milestones[] — should be elevated to its own top-level block kind for independent queryability + DEC-0013 edge-bearing
gaps.description: Milestone is the schema-shaped concept that matches "end-state-intention that organizes the work needing to be done" — every roadmap.milestones[] entry carries id (^MILESTONE-[A-Z0-9-]+$) + name + criterion + evidence_block + evidence_query. The third orchestrator query ("what must happen or have happened before X can be worked on and delivered") maps to traversing dependencies from a milestone backward through phases. Current shape (per packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/roadmap.schema.json properties.roadmaps.items.properties.milestones): milestones are INLINE within roadmap.milestones[], not a top-level block kind. Consequences: (1) MILESTONE-NNN ids cannot be resolved via filter-block-items / resolve-items-by-id / walk-ancestors / find-references — query primitives operate on top-level array blocks. (2) Resolving a milestone always goes through roadmap loading + array scan. (3) relations.json edges cannot canonically connect MILESTONE-NNN to FGAP-NNN / DEC-NNN / FEAT-NNN / VER-NNN as parent/child — the inline embedding forces edges to live as roadmap-internal arrays. (4) The roadmap.phases[].milestone FK and roadmap.milestones[] inline shape are both DEC-0013 violations — inter-item references via inline field/array. User direction 2026-05-14: elevate milestone to its own block kind.

evidence.reference: properties.roadmaps.items.properties.milestones — inline array within roadmap items; ^MILESTONE-[A-Z0-9-]+$ pattern declared but no top-level block kind
properties.roadmaps.items.properties.phases.items.properties.milestone — inline FK string from phase to milestone; DEC-0013 violation
Milestone resolution flows through loadRoadmap; no independent milestone-loading surface
PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES list at L117 excludes milestone — confirms block-kind absence

gaps.impact: Milestone is the natural anchor for the orchestrator query "what must happen before X is delivered" — X is a milestone, prerequisites are phases + tasks + decisions that gate it. Current inline shape blocks: (a) independent milestone-by-status lensing (milestone-by-status / milestone-by-evidence-availability rollups); (b) closure-table edges anchoring on MILESTONE-NNN; (c) gather-execution-context binding unit_kind=milestone for context bundle composition (CTX-NNN extension); (d) cross-roadmap milestone references (a milestone in roadmap A as gate for phase in roadmap B). Filable cascade to phases (FGAP-049 covers phase block-kind divergence) and to roadmap-shape (roadmap shape itself becomes inline-references-to-milestones + inline-references-to-phases via relations.json edges).
gaps.proposed_resolution: Elevate milestone to top-level block kind: (1) Author packages/pi-context/registry/schemas/milestone.schema.json with array_key=milestones, item shape carrying id (^MILESTONE-[A-Z0-9-]+$) + name + criterion + evidence_block + evidence_query + status + created_by + created_at + description + related_decisions[] + related_features[] + canonical_vocabulary (analogous to FGAP/DEC schema shape). Schema title="Milestones" per DEC-0023. (2) Author packages/pi-context/registry/blocks/milestones.json with empty milestones[]. (3) Register milestone in PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES in project-sdk.ts + adjust roadmap-plan.ts to resolve milestones via top-level block load rather than roadmap-internal embedded array.
