(4) Roadmap schema migration: roadmap.milestones[] becomes reference array of MILESTONE-NNN strings OR moves entirely to relations.json with relation_type=roadmap_contains_milestone per DEC-0013. roadmap.phases[].milestone FK moves to relations.json edge phase_culminates_at_milestone. (5) Cross-decision with FGAP-049 (phase block-kind pattern) — same elevation pattern likely applies; phase should also be array-block, not file-per-phase. (6) Install milestone schema in .project/schemas/ as part of TASK-042 + TASK-025 substrate cascade. Acceptance: filter-block-items --block milestones --where status=open works; walk-ancestors --id MILESTONE-001 returns prerequisite phases + decisions; find-references --id MILESTONE-001 surfaces every block that depends on it.

gaps.title: No CTX-NNN context-contract for unit_kind=milestone — gatherExecutionContext cannot compose milestone bundle for cascade query
gaps.description: DEC-0017 declares work-unit context is composed via bidirectional traversal of config-declared relation_types per unit-kind context contract. Current context-contracts (.project/context-contracts.json) ship 3 entries: CTX-001 task / CTX-002 decision / CTX-003 verification. No entry for unit_kind=milestone. Per DEC-0024 (milestones-as-declarative-organizing-anchor enacted), milestone is the primary entry point — "what's the picture around MILESTONE-NNN" is the canonical query for understanding a body of work + its decomposition + its satisfaction state. Without a milestone context-contract, gatherExecutionContext --unit-id MILESTONE-NNN returns degenerate (unit-only) bundle — no research / decisions / framework-gaps / features / phases / verifications cascade traversal. Plus: existing CTX-001/002/003 carry empty bundle_relation_types[] (registered but not populated) — same gap pattern at smaller scale.

evidence.reference: 3 contracts (CTX-001 task / CTX-002 decision / CTX-003 verification); none for milestone; all carry bundle_relation_types: []
gatherExecutionContext composes ContextBundle per declared context-contract; returns degenerate when bundle_relation_types[] empty or unit_kind absent
DEC-0024 enacts milestones-as-anchor model; cascade requires context-contract
Schema permits unit_kind plain-string keying; no per-kind whitelist

gaps.impact: Milestone-anchored work cannot be queried for full context via canonical primitive. LLM (Claude or in-pi) asking "compose context for MILESTONE-007" gets unit-only bundle. The third orchestrator query ("what must happen or have happened before X delivered") for X=milestone is partial — walk-ancestors works on edges if authored, but composed-context-bundle (gather-execution-context) is the unified surface and it can't compose milestone context. Cascade impact: every consumer that anchors on milestones (roadmap-plan satisfaction check; future curation surfaces; LLM-driven decomposition assistance) hits the same gap.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add CTX-NNN entry for unit_kind=milestone with bundle_relation_types covering: (a) milestone_grounded_by_research (in, depth 2) — research feeding the milestone; (b) milestone_addressed_by_decision (in, depth 2) — decisions enacted in service of the milestone; (c) milestone_blocked_by_gap (in, depth 2) — framework-gaps gating milestone satisfaction; (d) milestone_delivered_by_feature (in, depth 3) — features whose acceptance criteria contribute to satisfaction; (e) phase_culminates_at_milestone (in, depth 2) — phases that terminate at this milestone + their tasks via lens; (f) milestone_verified_by (in, depth 1) — verification entries cited as evidence; (g) milestone_depends_on (out, depth 2) — prerequisite milestones. relation_type canonical_ids settle as part of FGAP-047 vocabulary settlement. Once CTX-NNN authored, gatherExecutionContext --unit-id MILESTONE-NNN returns full cascade bundle. Cross-decision: similar context-contract extension warranted for unit_kind=feature + unit_kind=phase + unit_kind=roadmap once those blocks are first-class queryable per FGAP-053 + FGAP-049 + FGAP-055. Also: populate empty bundle_relation_types[] on existing CTX-001/002/003 as part of same arc.

gaps.title: Roadmap shape thinning post-milestone-elevation — what remains in roadmap when milestones + phases become top-level blocks
gaps.description: Per DEC-0024 (milestones-as-anchor enacted) + FGAP-053 (milestone elevation to top-level block) + FGAP-049 (phase pattern divergence; phase elevation likely follows), roadmap.milestones[] inline array + roadmap.phases[] inline array migrate to top-level milestone + phase blocks with closure-table edges. After migration, roadmap retains: id (ROADMAP-NNN), title, description, status (lifecycle: draft / active / paused / complete / archived), created_by, created_at. Substantive structure (which milestones, in what order, gated by which phases) lives in: top-level milestone block + top-level phase block + relations.json edges (relation_type: roadmap_contains_milestone, roadmap_contains_phase, phase_culminates_at_milestone, milestone_depends_on, phase_depends_on) + config.lenses[] composition lens spec for grouping. Roadmap becomes a thin envelope: a named, scoped, lifecycle-bearing curation. Open question about whether roadmap remains a block kind at all OR collapses into config.lenses[] (composition lens binning milestones-by-scope) — collapsing loses per-roadmap lifecycle status, which is the one substantive surface roadmap retains uniquely.

evidence.reference: Current shape: id + title + description + status + phases[] inline + milestones[] inline
DEC-0024 enacts milestones-as-anchor model
FGAP-053 milestone elevation; FGAP-049 phase pattern divergence
loadRoadmap currently reads inline arrays; post-elevation cascades to top-level block loads + edge resolution

gaps.impact: Roadmap schema migration + roadmap-plan.ts cascade are downstream of FGAP-053 + FGAP-049 elevation work. If not addressed coherently, roadmap remains carrying inline arrays parallel to elevated blocks, producing two sources of truth + drift potential. Migration touches: schema (roadmap.schema.json revised); registry default (roadmap.json blank instance shape changes); roadmap-plan.ts code (load + render flows); any consumer reading roadmap.phases / roadmap.milestones inline (currently roadmap-plan.ts + tests). Open design questions: (a) does roadmap remain a block kind or collapse into a lens; (b) if it remains, what's the minimum field set; (c) what relation_types canonically connect roadmap to its milestones/phases; (d) how does roadmap-plan render the cascade post-migration.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Resolution sequenced after FGAP-053 elevation lands: (1) Revise roadmap.schema.json — drop phases[] + milestones[] inline arrays; retain id + title + description + status + created_by + created_at; consider removing milestones field entirely OR repurposing as a backlog of ad-hoc-attached milestones not modeled by the canonical structure. (2) Author relation_types in config.relation_types[] — roadmap_contains_milestone (membership), roadmap_contains_phase (membership), phase_culminates_at_milestone (membership). (3) Update roadmap-plan.ts loadRoadmap to resolve milestones + phases via top-level block loads + edge traversals rather than inline. (4) Optional: define a composition lens (config.lenses[]) per roadmap to project member items via groupByLens; lens definition lives in config.lenses[], roadmap.id ties to lens.id. (5) Cross-decision with FGAP-053 + FGAP-049: this resolution lands AFTER both elevation arcs complete. (6) Could also evaluate whether roadmap collapses entirely into a lens — tradeoff: loses per-roadmap lifecycle status which is the unique surface roadmap retains; recommend keeping roadmap as thin block.

gaps.title: PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const at project-sdk.ts:117 hardcodes 12 default block kinds — should be config-derived for vocabulary-neutrality (DEC-0025)
gaps.description: packages/pi-context/src/project-sdk.ts lines 117-130 declares: export const PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES = ['project', 'domain', 'requirements', 'architecture', 'tasks', 'decisions', 'issues', 'rationale', 'verification', 'handoff', 'conformance-reference', 'audit'] as const. This list is consumed downstream as the canonical default block-kind set shipped with /project init. Per DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral substrate canon), built-in vocabulary literals in source are gaps to close — the framework's source code must operate on canonical_id-keyed registries derived purely from loaded config. A user with a wholly different substrate conception (e.g. {initiatives, OKRs, experiments, decisions, tasks, runs, observations}) cannot use pi-context as-is without either (a) accepting these 12 defaults as installed even when irrelevant or (b) modifying source to remove or replace them. The const is also a regression risk: any future addition to the const adds an implicit framework commitment.

evidence.reference: Lines 117-130: PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const with 12 hardcoded canonical_ids
Line 1707: re-exports PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES (consumed by /project init + tooling)
DEC-0025 declares vocabulary-neutrality principle; this const violates it
Default block instances shipped under /registry — those are the proper home for shipped defaults; the const duplicates their listing

gaps.impact: Vocabulary-neutrality claim of DEC-0025 partially undelivered. User cannot adopt pi-context with wholly-different substrate conception purely through config + schemas + macros — the const remains as a framework default-vocabulary commitment in source. Tools relying on PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES (e.g. /project init scaffolding) cannot scaffold a fully-custom substrate without code change. Cross-package consumers (pi-workflows, pi-behavior-monitors, pi-jit-agents) that reference PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES inherit the same coupling.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Replace PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const with config-derived equivalent: (a) /project init (or future /context init) reads packages/pi-context/registry/blocks/ + registry/schemas/ at install time + lets user select which to install via prompt or flag; (b) downstream tooling that iterates PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES instead reads from loaded config.installed_blocks[]; (c) the const, if retained, becomes a documentation-only export naming the canonical-default kinds shipped under registry — not a source-of-truth for what is installed. Cascade: audit all PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES consumers (grep across packages/* + scripts/*); each consumer migrates to config.installed_blocks[] read OR registry directory enumeration; tests updated to exercise non-default substrate vocabularies (FGAP-058). Cross-decision with FGAP-052 (config-declared-but-unconsumed runtime gaps) — same root principle.

gaps.title: Macro library bootstrap missing for user-added block kinds — framework provides canonical kinds' macros only; user-added kind has no scaffolding for vocabulary-neutral substrate
gaps.description: Per DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral substrate canon), user must be able to author any block kind via config + schema + macro and have pi-context's machinery operate on it. packages/pi-workflows/templates/shared/macros.md ships per-block-kind Nunjucks render macros for canonical kinds (decisions / framework-gaps / etc.); FGAP-037 tracks 6 missing macros for canonical kinds. Beyond that gap: user-added block kinds (e.g. 'initiatives' / 'OKRs' / 'experiments' for a different substrate conception) have no canonical scaffolding pattern — no documentation of macro shape, no template generator, no auto-fallback rendering for kinds without dedicated macros. Result: even if user installs custom block kinds via config + schemas, prompt-injection rendering (block injection per DEC-0017 contextBlocks pattern) cannot proceed without authoring per-kind macros. Framework's vocabulary-neutrality claim is partially undelivered at the prompt-substrate layer.

evidence.reference: Per-block-kind render macros — only for canonical kinds; FGAP-037 enumerates 6 missing among canonical set
FGAP-037 macro library coverage gap for canonical kinds; FGAP-057 extends to user-added kinds
contextBlocks injection assumes macro per block kind exists; no fallback for user-added kinds
DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutral substrate canon principle — implies macro library must support arbitrary user-defined kinds

gaps.impact: User adopting pi-context with non-default substrate conception cannot inject their custom block kinds into agent prompts via the canonical contextBlocks pattern without authoring macros per-kind. No documentation guides what macro shape to author. No fallback render exists when macro absent (silent fail or template error). Vocabulary-neutrality is partially blocked at the prompt-substrate layer; downstream effect: in-pi LLM operating on user-defined substrate cannot receive context-block-injected prompts for those user kinds.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Three-part: (a) Document the canonical macro shape — what fields a render macro must accept (block items array; injection delimiters; budget hints); add a 'authoring custom block macros' section to skill-narratives + CLAUDE.md. (b) Auto-discovery — pi-jit-agents compile-time looks for macros in three-tier search (.pi/templates → user templates → package); user drops a macro file matching kind name and framework picks it up. Already partially implemented per CLAUDE.md three-tier mention; verify cascade works for user-added kinds. (c) Fallback render — when no macro exists for a kind, framework auto-generates a minimal render (item iteration + key-value output) with a warning surfaced via execution-context diagnostics; prevents silent fail. Cross-decision with FGAP-037 (canonical-kind macro coverage) — same workstream extends to user-added kind support.

gaps.title: Test suite doesn't exercise pi-context against a fully-different substrate vocabulary — vocabulary-neutrality claim per DEC-0025 untested
gaps.description: Per DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral substrate canon), a user with wholly-different substrate conception (e.g. {initiatives, OKRs, experiments, decisions, tasks, runs, observations} OR {hypotheses, experiments, results, papers, datasets} OR any other vocabulary) must be able to clone pi-context, author their own config + schemas + macros, and have the framework operate on their vocabulary without source modification. This claim is currently untested. pi-context's existing test suite (390 tests) exercises the framework against canonical-default vocabulary (decisions / framework-gaps / tasks / etc.) — every test fixture authors substrate using PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES kinds. No test instantiates a fully-different substrate conception + exercises block-api / lens / closure-table / query primitives / execution-context against it. Result: claim is asserted (in DEC-0025) but unevidenced. Any latent coupling between pi-context source and the canonical-default vocabulary remains undiscovered until a real user attempts a vocabulary-neutral adoption + finds the gap.

evidence.reference: All 390 tests use canonical-default vocabulary fixtures — grep for 'decisions' / 'framework-gaps' / 'tasks' in test files surfaces canonical-only usage
DEC-0025 enacts vocabulary-neutrality; no test evidence for the claim
FGAP-056 (PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES const) + FGAP-057 (macro bootstrap) — the surfaces a vocabulary-neutral test would exercise + likely fail against currently

gaps.impact: Vocabulary-neutrality claim is asserted but unevidenced. Latent couplings between pi-context source and canonical-default vocabulary remain hidden — any code path that hardcodes a kind name, prefix, relation_type literal, lens id, or layer label is a regression that won't surface until a downstream user attempts vocabulary-neutral adoption + reports failure. Adversarial discoverability low: standard test pass-rate doesn't catch coupling drift. Per DEC-0018 (tests-pass-alone insufficient; runtime demo + adversarial probe per step), this is a verification gap at the framework-claim level.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Author a fixture-based test that: (a) creates a tmpdir with .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer naming a custom dir (e.g. .lab/); (b) populates .lab/config.json + .lab/schemas/ + .lab/<custom-kinds>.json with a wholly-different substrate vocabulary (e.g. {hypotheses, experiments, results, datasets, papers}); (c) exercises block-api appendToBlock / readBlock / upsertItemInBlock against custom kinds; (d) declares custom relation_types in config.relation_types[] + populates .lab/relations.json with edges; (e) declares custom lens in config.lenses[] + verifies groupByLens projection works; (f) authors custom macro under .lab/templates/ + verifies pi-jit-agents contextBlocks injection picks it up; (g) calls gather-execution-context for a custom unit_kind via custom CTX-NNN context-contract + verifies bundle composition. Test failure cases reveal coupling sites; each becomes a code-path migration to vocabulary-neutral pattern. Lives at packages/pi-context/src/vocabulary-neutrality.integration.test.ts (analogous to execution-context.integration.test.ts which exists per Phase 3 Step 8.7.3.3). Cross-decision with FGAP-056 + FGAP-057 — those gaps surface DURING this test authoring; their resolution gates this test passing.

gaps.title: No canonical primitive for "atomic-next-work-unit" query — existing filter / walk-ancestors / find-references require caller composition; substrate cannot self-direct
gaps.description: The operational test of the substrate canon is the query "what SHOULD we / must we do next, atomically, given current state?". To answer canonically, the framework must compose: (a) gating open DECs related to active work — user enactment gates everything downstream; (b) blocking framework-gaps with status=identified + priority P1 referenced by active work; (c) frontier of unblocked tasks — tasks status=planned whose depends_on (closure-table edges) are all completed; (d) in-progress tasks already active; (e) sort by dependency depth + priority + return minimal frontier. Currently each ingredient exists as a separate primitive: filter-block-items handles status filters; walk-ancestors traverses dependencies (when relations.json populated); find-references surfaces cross-references. None composes the answer end-to-end. Caller must orchestrate manually. Result: substrate cannot self-direct — neither the harness-confined LLM (DEC-0014) nor an external orchestrator can ask a single canonical primitive "what's next" and receive a deterministic answer. Operational outcome of all substrate canon work is gated on this composition primitive.

evidence.reference: filterBlockItems / walkAncestors / findReferences exist as separate primitives
gatherExecutionContext composes per-unit context bundle but does NOT compute frontier or atomic-next
Roadmap status rollup exists but does not project to atomic-next-work answer
DEC-0014 harness-confined LLM cannot navigate canonically without this primitive; DEC-0024 milestone-as-anchor model presupposes it
No orchestrator script composes the atomic-next query; each invocation is a standalone primitive

gaps.impact: Operational evidence of substrate canon delivery is gated on this primitive. Without it: (a) harness-confined LLM (DEC-0014) cannot operate canonically — it must improvise queries or rely on external tooling; (b) the third orchestrator query ("what must happen before X delivered") for active work returns partial info — caller must orchestrate; (c) status reporting (HANDOFF refresh; what's-the-current-focus) requires manual composition every time; (d) milestone-as-anchor model (DEC-0024) provides aspirational anchors but no canonical frontier projection; (e) any future ceremony like /context status or /context what-next that aims to surface actionable next-work has no underlying primitive to call.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add a composition primitive nextAtomicWork(cwd, options?) in packages/pi-context/src/ (separate file, e.g. atomic-next.ts; or extension to project-sdk.ts). Algorithm: (1) Load all open DECs with related_gaps/features intersecting active milestones/features → return as P0 frontier (user enactment required first). (2) If empty, load identified FGAPs P1 referenced by active work → return as P1 frontier. (3) If empty, walk relations.json for tasks status=planned with all upstream depends_on completed → return as P2 frontier. (4) If empty, return in-progress tasks. (5) Sort each tier by ordinal/priority/created_at. Returns FrontierBundle { p0: openDecs, p1: blockingGaps, p2: unblockedPlanned, p3: inProgress }. Cross-decision with FGAP-046 (depends_on inline arrays migration to closure-table) — frontier query operates on relations.json edges; inline arrays don't traverse. Also add: pi-registered tool 'next-atomic-work'; orchestrator script scripts/orchestrator/next-atomic-work.ts; consumed by HANDOFF auto-refresh + future ceremonies. Acceptance: a single npx tsx scripts/orchestrator/next-atomic-work.ts (or pi -p "call next-atomic-work") returns the minimal actionable frontier given current substrate state, deterministic across runs, derivable purely from substrate (no improvisation).

gaps.title: No automatic cross-file value-rename engine for canonical_id changes — schema-migrations handles shape, not canonical_id value renames across relations.json edges + item-id-prefix renumbering
gaps.description:
The framework foresees + carries SCHEMA-SHAPE migration via schema-migrations.ts (MigrationRegistry + runMigrations + validateBlockWithMigration at schema-validator.ts:204): a block file declaring schema_version 1.0.0 migrates forward to current via registered MigrationFn chain on read. This handles field renames / restructures / add-with-default WITHIN a block's item shape. It does NOT handle canonical_id VALUE renames that span files. Two cases: (1) relation_type canonical_id rename — relations.json edges store the relation_type canonical_id (relations.schema.json: edge.relation_type = 'registered relation_type canonical_id'); renaming a relation_type's canonical_id requires rewriting every edge referencing it; no MigrationFn covers cross-file value rewrites; runMigrations operates per-block-file data shape only. (2) item-ID-prefix renumbering (DEC-0026 padding / DEC-0027 casing) — item canonical_id (e.g. DEC-0001, issue-001) EMBEDS the prefix; unlike relation_types (which split stable canonical_id from mutable display_name per config.relation_types[]), item ids ARE the reference with no id/label split, so renaming the prefix requires renumbering every item + every citation across relations.json edges + related_X fields + analysis MDs + commit messages.

CRITICAL MITIGATION ALREADY DESIGNED: the canonical_id <-> display_name decoupling (config.relation_types[].canonical_id vs display_name; config.block_kinds[].canonical_id vs display_name per DEC-0023) means HUMAN-LABEL renames are FREE — edge/reference holds stable canonical_id; label lives in display_name; relabel = display_name edit = live, zero migration. The value-rename gap applies ONLY to canonical_id-itself changes, which are rare by design (primary-key semantics: set once, treat permanent). [2026-05-20 CLOSED — unit 2.3, commit 33b76a7: renameCanonicalId engine landed for 4 canonical_id kinds (item, relation_type, lens, layer) + dual surface (rename-canonical-id Pi tool + scripts/orchestrator/migrate-canonical-id.ts) + writeRelations/writeConfig wrappers + out-of-substrate report (analysis grep + best-effort git log -S, report-only) + dry-run. The two cases this FGAP named — relation_type canonical_id rename + item-id-prefix renumbering — are both delivered, as are lens/layer. block_kind canonical_id rename (the file/data_path/array_key/schema_path cascade) is the one remaining sliver, tracked as FGAP-071.]

evidence.reference: MigrationRegistry + runMigrations operate per-block-file data shape (currentVersion → targetVersion); no cross-file value-rename surface
validateBlockWithMigration:204 — read-time forward migration of one block file's data; does not touch other files' references
edge.relation_type stores registered relation_type canonical_id — renaming the canonical_id orphans existing edges
relation_types[].canonical_id + display_name split (lines 70-84) — enables free label rename; canonical_id rename is the migration-cost case
DEC-0026 + DEC-0027 flag item-id-prefix renumbering churn; DEC-0030 relation_type naming shape

gaps.impact: When a vocabulary decision is rethought AFTER substrate is populated: human-label revisions are free (display_name edit) but canonical_id revisions have no automatic engine. relation_type canonical_id rename orphans relations.json edges silently (validateRelations may flag dangling refs but doesn't auto-rewrite). item-id-prefix renumbering (DEC-0026/0027) cascades across every reference site with no tooling. The schema-shape migration foresight (FGAP-006, closed) has no value-rename counterpart. Practically: deciding canonical_ids well initially matters because canonical_id is primary-key-permanent; display_names are the cheap-revision surface. Gap is the absence of a migration primitive for the rare-but-real canonical_id-rename case.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Two-part: (a) Document + enforce the canonical_id-is-permanent discipline — canonical_ids (relation_type / block_kind / lens / layer) set once, never renamed; human-facing changes go through display_name. Add to CLAUDE.md substrate canon + schema descriptions. This makes case (1) a non-event by convention. (b) For the genuine canonical_id-rename + item-id-prefix-renumbering cases that remain, author a value-rename migration primitive (e.g. scripts/orchestrator/migrate-canonical-id.ts + library renameCanonicalId(cwd, kind, oldId, newId)) that: rewrites the canonical_id in its home block; rewrites every relations.json edge referencing it (parent + child + relation_type fields); rewrites every related_X field across all blocks that cite it; reports citation sites in analysis MDs + commit messages it cannot auto-rewrite (out-of-substrate). Dry-run mode lists affected sites before write. For item-id-prefix renumbering (DEC-0026/0027), the same primitive batch-renames id prefixes + cascades references. Cross-decision with DEC-0026/0027 (prefix conventions) — settling those AND building this primitive together avoids a second renumbering later.

gaps.title: config.block_kinds[] requires single array_key per block — cannot represent mixed-shape blocks (project / architecture / domain / conformance-reference) with multiple arrays + scalar identity fields
gaps.description: config.schema.json block_kinds[] items require [canonical_id, display_name, prefix, schema_path, array_key, data_path] — array_key is a single required string naming THE top-level array property in the block JSON. This models clean array-blocks (decisions.decisions[] / framework-gaps.gaps[] / tasks.tasks[] / etc.) correctly. It cannot represent mixed-shape blocks: project.json (scalar name/description/core_value/status + goals[] + constraints[] + scope_boundaries{}), architecture.json (modules[] + patterns[] + boundaries[] + compilation_pipeline{} + execution_model{} + dispatch_design{}), domain.json (entries[] + possibly scalars), conformance-reference.json (name + scope{} + principles[]). These have ZERO or MULTIPLE top-level arrays + scalar identity fields with no single canonical array_key. Surfaced twice: build-html-views.ts agent hit it (discoverArrayKey picks largest array, discards scalars — lossy); config.json authoring (FGAP-046/060 arc Phase 1) cannot author block_kinds[] entries for these blocks. The step-1 config draft (analysis/2026-05-14-step-1-config-draft-from-substrate.md) papered over it by putting scalar field 'name' as project's array_key, which is incorrect.

evidence.reference: block_kinds[].array_key required single string (lines 44-52)
scalar name/description/core_value/status + goals[] + constraints[] + scope_boundaries{} — no single array_key
modules[] + patterns[] + boundaries[] + 3 object fields — multiple arrays
discoverArrayKey heuristic + identity-panel workaround built precisely because config.block_kinds can't declare mixed-shape
draft put scalar 'name' as project array_key — incorrect, surfaces the gap

gaps.impact: config.json cannot losslessly declare all installed blocks. Mixed-shape blocks (project / architecture / domain / conformance-reference) are unrepresentable in block_kinds[]. Any consumer iterating config.block_kinds[] for block enumeration misses them OR mis-declares their shape. Forces per-consumer heuristics (build-html-views discoverArrayKey). For the FGAP-046/060 arc: not blocking (cross-reference-bearing blocks are all clean single-array_key array-blocks), but config.block_kinds[] authored for this arc will omit the mixed-shape blocks, leaving config incomplete.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Extend config.block_kinds[] schema: replace required single 'array_key' with optional 'array_keys: string[]' (zero or more top-level array properties) + optional 'identity_fields: string[]' (top-level scalar/object fields forming block identity). Single-array blocks declare array_keys: ['decisions']; mixed-shape blocks declare array_keys: ['goals','constraints'] + identity_fields: ['name','description','core_value','status','scope_boundaries']; pure-singleton blocks declare array_keys: []. Migrate existing consumers (project-sdk availableBlocks / blockStructure; build-html-views discoverArrayKey → read config.block_kinds[].array_keys; schema-validator). Schema version bump + MigrationFn (array_key string → array_keys[1-element array]). Cross-decision with the build-html-views identity-panel rendering (already handles multi-array + identity via schemaInfo introspection — config.block_kinds[] should carry the same shape so introspection isn't re-derived per consumer).

gaps.title: expectedBlockForId treats empty prefix as match-everything — empty-prefix block_kind becomes a catch-all that breaks buildIdIndex invariant
gaps.description: expectedBlockForId (project-sdk.ts:670) resolves an item id to its expected block by finding the block_kind whose prefix the id startsWith, longest-prefix-wins. An empty prefix '' matches EVERY id (every string startsWith ''), so a block_kind declaring prefix:'' becomes a catch-all claiming every unprefixed id. buildIdIndex (project-sdk.ts:754) then throws when it encounters a freeform-slug id (e.g. conventions rule 'esm') in its real block: expectedBlockForId returns the empty-prefix block (e.g. requirements) ≠ actual block (conventions) → 'Prefix-vs-block-kind invariant violated' hard throw. Discovered during FGAP-046/060 arc config.json authoring: declaring requirements (empty prefix, zero items) + conventions (freeform-slug ids, empty prefix) in block_kinds[] made requirements a catch-all for conventions' 'esm'/'tsc-build'/'no-pi-dir' ids. Workaround: dropped both empty-prefix blocks from block_kinds. Root: expectedBlockForId should treat empty prefix as 'opt out of prefix-resolution' (return null / never-match), NOT match-all.

evidence.reference: expectedBlockForId:670 — `if (id.startsWith(bk.prefix))` with bk.prefix='' matches all ids
buildIdIndex:754-760 — throws on expected!==blockName; empty-prefix catch-all triggers false invariant violation
freeform-slug ids esm / tsc-build / no-pi-dir — no prefix scheme; collide with any empty-prefix block

gaps.impact: Any config.block_kinds[] declaring two or more empty-prefix blocks (or one empty-prefix block alongside a freeform-slug block) hard-throws validateProject / resolveItemById / buildIdIndex. Blocks vocabulary-neutral adoption (DEC-0025) for substrates with freeform-slug-id blocks (conventions-shaped) or prefix-less blocks (project/architecture/domain shaped). Forces dropping such blocks from block_kinds[], leaving them unrepresented (compounds FGAP-061 + FGAP-051).
gaps.proposed_resolution: expectedBlockForId: skip block_kinds with empty prefix (treat '' as 'no prefix-based resolution declared' → never the expected block; return null when only empty-prefix would match). Add a unit test: freeform-slug id + empty-prefix block in config → expectedBlockForId returns null → buildIdIndex indexes the item under its actual block without throwing. Cross-decision with FGAP-051 (conventions freeform-slug-id) + FGAP-061 (block-shape declaration) — freeform-slug-id blocks may warrant an explicit id_scheme: 'slug' | 'prefixed' declaration in config.block_kinds[] rather than overloading empty-prefix. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 — commit 4fb4d1e / TASK-062 / VER-034: expectedBlockForId skips empty-prefix block_kinds (no catch-all). Empty prefix is legitimate (slug-id blocks e.g. conventions, FGAP-051) — schema unchanged (1.4.0, no minLength; an initial minLength:1 was reverted as it broke the conception). Slug-id blocks are excluded from prefix-based resolution.]

gaps.title: Orchestration / procedural-policy canon has no dedicated substrate block — scattered across decisions (procedural DECs), conventions (coding-only), feedback memories (off-substrate), and the mandate hook
gaps.description: Two senses of orchestration land in different places. (1) Orchestration MACHINERY (workflow execution / agent dispatch / monitor steering) is engineering-layer L4 dispatch — pi-workflows + pi-jit-agents + pi-behavior-monitors; that is code, correctly placed. (2) Orchestration as PROCEDURAL POLICY — how work is conducted: the operating pattern (plan → substrate-write → dispatch → verify → commit → status-cascade), verify-before-relay, runtime-demo+adversarial-probe, status-updates-after-each-step, foreground-subagent dispatch, plan-mode-per-phase. This procedural/methodology canon is NOT a work-altitude (roadmap/milestone/phase/task) and NOT an engineering code-layer; it is a cross-cutting concern governing HOW the other layers are operated. It currently has no unified substrate home — it is split across four surfaces: (a) decisions block — procedural DECs (DEC-0018 runtime-demo+adversarial-probe; DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface; DEC-0021 atomic-cascade) conflated with architectural DECs (DEC-0013/0015) in the same block with no facet distinguishing structural-canon from procedural-canon; (b) conventions block — scoped to coding/build policy only (esm / tsc-build / no-pi-dir), does not cover process; (c) feedback memories under ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/feedback_*.md — behavioral mandates (verify-before-relay / no-background-subagents / status-updates-after-each-step) living entirely OFF substrate; (d) the mandate hook (mandate-001..009) — hard procedural constraints enforced at the harness, also off substrate. A reader (human or harness-confined LLM per DEC-0014) asking 'what is the operating procedure for conducting work in this project' has no single queryable substrate surface; they must assemble it from procedural-DECs + off-substrate feedback memories + the mandate hook. This is the FGAP-051 finding (conventions underspecified) at a higher altitude: conventions covers coding policy; orchestration/process policy has no block at all.

evidence.reference: DEC-0018/0019/0020/0021 are procedural/methodology canon filed in the same decisions block as architectural DEC-0013/0015 with no facet separating the two kinds
rules are coding/build-scoped (esm / tsc-build / no-pi-dir / lint); no process/dispatch/verification entries

feedback memories (verify-before-relay / no-background-subagents / status-updates-after-each-step / plan-mode-step-one-substrate-write) live off-substrate in ~/.claude memory; not queryable via block-api; not part of the dogfood substrate
Completion Sequence + operating pattern documented in prose, not as queryable substrate items

Tier B methodology canon recognized as a distinct concern; analysis/2026-04-15-process-articulation.md maps the ten-phase unit-of-work lifecycle — process is a known but under-modeled substrate concern

gaps.impact: The harness-confined main LLM (DEC-0014) operating purely through substrate cannot query the operating procedure — the methodology that governs its own conduct is off-substrate (feedback memories + mandate hook) or buried in undifferentiated DECs. Procedural canon cannot be lensed (process-by-category), edge-linked (which DEC enacts which procedural rule), or version-tracked as a coherent body. New contributors/LLMs reconstruct the operating pattern from scattered prose. The vocabulary-neutral substrate vision (DEC-0025) — user bootstraps their whole conception from config + schemas — is incomplete: a user adopting pi-context gets block kinds for work-content but no surface for their own process/methodology canon. Conflation of procedural + architectural DECs in one block prevents distinguishing 'how the system is built' from 'how work is conducted'.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Two candidate paths (user decides): (A) New block kind `process` / `protocol` / `methodology` (PROC-NNN) holding procedural-policy items (operating pattern, dispatch discipline, verification protocol, status-cascade) — each with enforcement (hook / review / manual) + applies_to + related_decisions edges; migrate the procedural feedback memories + procedural-DEC content into it as queryable substrate; the mandate hook references PROC-NNN ids. (B) Extend conventions block (DEC-0034, open) with a `category` facet (coding / build / process / dispatch / verification) so conventions spans both coding policy AND procedural policy; procedural feedback memories migrate in as category:process rules. Path B reuses an existing block + aligns with DEC-0034's already-proposed conventions migration; Path A gives process its own first-class block (cleaner separation, more authoring surface). Either path: (1) distinguish procedural canon from architectural canon (a DEC facet `kind: architectural | procedural` on decisions, OR moving procedural DECs to the new surface); (2) bring off-substrate feedback memories on-substrate as queryable items (the mandate hook + feedback_*.md become substrate-derived rather than parallel ungated surfaces — relates to feedback_no_stderr_diagnostics / parallel-ungated-path discipline); (3) edge-link procedural rules to the architectural DECs that force them. Cross-decision with DEC-0034 (conventions schema migration) + FGAP-051 (conventions underspec).

gaps.title: No top-level user-story block — stories exist only nested inside features[].stories[]; cannot relate independently to project-goal / milestone / phase / task via closure-table
gaps.description: Stories currently exist ONLY as a nested inline array inside features (.project/schemas/features.schema.json definitions.story; features[].stories[]; 9 STORY-NNN instances under FEAT-001). There is no top-level story / user-story block: not a file in .project/, not in config.block_kinds, not independently queryable via filter-block-items / walk-ancestors / find-references / resolve-items-by-id. Consequence: a story cannot be related independently to project-goal (global), milestone, phase, or task — it is locked inside its parent feature. The envisioned flow (a project deliberates and produces N user-stories, then relates them across altitudes — global / milestone / phase / task) is not expressible because the story has no standalone identity to anchor closure-table edges. The framework CAN bear the conception: this is the identical elevation pattern as milestone (FGAP-053, milestone-inline-in-roadmap → top-level block) and phase (DEC-0028, file-per-phase → array-block). A top-level user-story block + relations.json edges expresses all the envisioned cross-altitude relations under DEC-0013 (closure-table allows one story to participate in multiple parallel hierarchies — milestone AND phase AND N tasks simultaneously). Sub-shape (whether the elevated block carries the Agile user-story form role/want/benefit, or stays structural like the current nested story title/status/tasks, or both coexist as distinct kinds) is DELIBERATELY LEFT OPEN here — to be designed when the gap is taken up, not presumed now.

evidence.reference: definitions.story nested in features; story id is plain string (no canonical pattern); fields id/title/status/description/acceptance_criteria/depends_on/gates/tasks
FEAT-001 carries 9 stories nested in stories[] (STORY-001..009); no standalone story block
block_kinds has no story/user-story entry
FGAP-053 (milestone elevation) is the parallel pattern; same nested-inline → top-level-block + edges shape

gaps.impact: Stories cannot anchor cross-altitude relations. Project deliberation producing a backlog of user-stories has no substrate home for them as first-class items. Stories cannot be lensed (stories-by-status / stories-by-milestone), edge-linked (story_serves_milestone / phase_contains_story / task_implements_story / project_goal_served_by_story), or context-composed (gather-execution-context unit_kind=story). The feature→story relationship is currently a DEC-0013 violation in waiting (nested inline child) the same way milestone-in-roadmap is — elevating story makes feature_contains_story a closure-table edge. Without elevation, the requirements→user-story→task traceability chain (and any user-story-driven planning) is unrepresentable.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Elevate story to a top-level array-block (parallel to FGAP-053 milestone elevation + DEC-0028 phase array-block): new block kind (canonical_id + prefix TBD at design time) with STORY-NNN-style ids; register in config.block_kinds; relations.json edges across altitudes via DEC-0030-tripartite relation_types (candidates, named at design time: project_goal_served_by_story / milestone_delivered_by_story / phase_contains_story / feature_contains_story / task_implements_story); migrate FEAT-001's 9 nested stories out of features[].stories[] into the top-level block + feature_contains_story edges; CTX-NNN context-contract for unit_kind=story. SUB-SHAPE NOT PRESUMED: whether the block is the Agile user-story form (role / want / benefit / acceptance_criteria), the current structural story form (title / status / tasks), or two distinct kinds — is an open design decision to resolve when this gap is taken up, gated on user deliberation. Cross-decision with FGAP-053 (milestone) + FGAP-046 (inline-nested elimination) + DEC-0013/0024/0025 — same elevation discipline.

gaps.title: validateProject invariants are hardcoded (completed-task-verification, decision-forcing-artifact) — DEC-0025 violation; must be config-declared
gaps.description: validateProject (project-sdk.ts ~860-975, machinery unit 2.1 / commit 73e568d) hardcodes two substrate invariants: (1) completed-task-has-verification — every task status=completed requires ≥1 verification_verifies_item edge (child=task); (2) decision-cites-forcing-artifact — every decision requires ≥1 decision_addresses_{issue,feature,gap} edge (parent=decision). These name specific block kinds (tasks, decisions), a specific status (completed), and specific relation_type canonical_ids — all hardcoded in the validator. Per DEC-0025 (substrate canon is vocabulary-neutral; a user's block/relation/status conception bootstraps entirely from config + schemas + macros without code changes), a user adopting the framework with a different conception (decisions that don't need forcing artifacts; tasks that don't need verification; entirely different block kinds) is forced into pi-context's specific invariants, unconfigurably. Surfaced during machinery unit 2.1: the unit-2.1 guard-flip fix (relations.length>0 → if config) extended these hardcoded invariants from dormant-on-zero-edge to firing on EVERY config-present substrate, sharpening the latent DEC-0025 violation. The invariants must be CONFIG-DECLARED (config.invariants[]) + validateProject must consume them generically (no hardcoded block/status/relation literals). Design established at analysis/2026-05-17-config-declared-invariants-design.md.

evidence.reference: validateProject hardcoded invariant 1 (completed-task-verification) ~928-945 + invariant 2 (decision-forcing-artifact) ~947-975 — block/status/relation_type literals hardcoded
DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutral substrate canon — source ships zero vocabulary commitment; invariants must be config-declared
config has no invariants[] declaration surface; lens 'where' field-equality (lines 124-130) is the reusable predicate precedent
established design: config.invariants[] requires-edge class + generic consumer + field-equality where + config-data defaults

gaps.impact: validateProject enforces pi-context-specific invariants on every config-present substrate. A vocabulary-neutral adopter (DEC-0025) with a different conception cannot escape the decision-forcing-artifact + completed-task-verification rules without modifying source. Blocks .context re-derivation (DEC-0036): re-population satisfies invariants at authoring time + needs config.invariants[] declared before validateProject gates it — if invariants stay hardcoded, the re-populated .context is forced into pi-context's specific invariants rather than its own declared ones. Sequencing: this fix must land before .context re-population.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Per analysis/2026-05-17-config-declared-invariants-design.md (all 3 flagged forks resolved as derivable: requires-edge-class-only [anti-over-engineering/YAGNI]; scaffold-placed config-data defaults [via-negativa + DEC-0025]; error severity default [preserve current behavior]): (1) add config.invariants[] to config.schema.json — requires-edge class {id, class, block, where?:{field:value}, relation_types[], direction:as_parent|as_child, severity?, message?}; (2) ConfigBlock type + loadConfig carry invariants; (3) validateProject replaces the 2 hardcoded invariant blocks with a generic loop over config.invariants ?? [] (zero hardcoded block/status/relation literals; reuses idIndex + relations; field-equality where predicate reusing the lens-composition precedent, NOT expression.ts — pi-context has no dep on pi-workflows expression.ts, importing inverts layering); (4) declare the 2 canonical invariants in .project/config.json + the /context-init scaffold default config (config-data, user-overridable; source ships zero invariant vocabulary → DEC-0025-clean); (5) tests: generic consumer fires per declared invariant incl. ABSENCE case (missing required edge → error) per the unit-2.1 false-pass lesson. Lands as a machinery unit before .context re-population (DEC-0036 step 3).

gaps.title: No conversational onboarding for substrate init — /context init should elicit the user’s own conception (block kinds / layers / relation types / lenses) with sample suggestions + revisable labels, rather than imposing a fixed default set.
gaps.description: A fresh adopter currently has no guided path to a substrate conception. Intended outcome: running /context init guides the user conversationally to declare THEIR conception — what work-items they track (becoming block_kinds: canonical_id + prefix + display label + shape), their work-altitudes (becoming layers), how items relate (becoming relation_types), and projections (becoming lenses) — with the canonical pi-context conception offered as SAMPLE SUGGESTIONS (a catalog menu), never imposed. Output is a valid config.json composed from the answers, written through the canonical config-write surface. Labels are revisable afterward (display_name relabel; canonical_id rename is the rare deliberate engine op). User framing: flexibility (their conception, not ours) + no-hardcoding (ship nothing, offer everything) + revisability. Two elicitation tiers fall out naturally: (a) pick-a-sample-and-relabel, (b) author-bespoke-fields. Mechanisms already present in-repo: pi.sendMessage follow-up-turn ceremony pattern (lens-curate at index.ts:1524; the harness-confined main LLM conducts the dialogue and writes via a registered tool, DEC-0014-clean); config registries already drive block_kinds/layers/relation_types/lenses (DEC-0025); config.naming alias map + display_name fields; schema-write.ts surface for bespoke schema authoring; the FGAP-060 rename engine for canonical_id rename. This also reframes registry/ from auto-installed defaults into the SUGGESTION CATALOG that onboarding offers — the honest role under DEC-0025 (a menu, not a mandate); pairs with the initProject defaults-imposition gap (sibling FGAP). [2026-05-20 refinement: settled as DEC-0037 (samples = this project's platonic PACKAGED conception — schema + relations + invariants + lens per kind — dogfooded; registry/ -> samples/) + DEC-0038 (onboarding = standing incremental registry-population surfaces, init = first pass, modes accept-all | step-through). Write-side mechanism = per-registry register + relabel surfaces; the canonical_id-revise leg is the FGAP-060 rename engine; the samples discovery/enumeration surface is FGAP-068. Bespoke net-new-kind authoring is out of scope (ship + subset the platonic conception first).]

evidence.reference: pi.sendMessage follow-up-turn ceremony precedent (lens-curate) — the established mechanism a conversational /context init onboarding would reuse; main LLM conducts dialogue, writes via registered tool (DEC-0014-clean)

DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral: a user’s own conception bootstraps from config), DEC-0011 (ship no defaults), DEC-0035 (display_name mutable / canonical_id permanent), DEC-0023 (schema title is display_name source), DEC-0029 (/context surface) — the enacted canon this onboarding operationalizes
existing ceremony catalog covers item-level ceremonies (new/edit/archive) only; onboarding-init elicitation is NOT covered — this is net-new canon

gaps.impact: Without it, every adopter either inherits an imposed conception (DEC-0011/0025 violation, see sibling FGAP) or hand-authors config.json with no guidance — the central DEC-0025 promise (a user’s own vocabulary bootstraps from config) has no on-ramp. Gates the /context surface (FGAP-026 Phase 6) being genuinely usable by a fresh adopter.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Deliver a /context start step-through onboarding that elicits + suggests + writes a config.json the user owns and can relabel, with the canonical conception (samples) as one selectable sample. Outcome-only filing: the implementer MUST first survey the pi-sdk surface (sendMessage / registerTool / before_agent_start / resources_discover / follow-up-turn) + prior art in other pi extensions, and adopt the exemplar pattern rather than invent one. SCOPE (FGAP-095/DEC-0042): this is the conversational STEP-THROUGH interior of /context start (the conductor's NO_CONFIG fork = accept-all | step-through); it supplies the step-through branch — do not silently substitute accept-all. PREREQUISITE + LEARNING-MECHANISM: the guided path requires the LLM to comprehend the framework to elicit/explain/suggest (else it confabulates the conception, FGAP-092). Mechanism = exact adherence to pi's self-docs hooks (FGAP-090): a before_agent_start eager pointer block carrying a topic->tool-call map (e.g. 'add a block kind -> read-samples-catalog, then write-schema + amend-config') + resources_discover framework-skill metadata + lazy content via element-read tools (FGAP-103). The two real entries are accept-all vs guided-conversational; raw-empty is the technical default (DEC-0011/0015), never a user-facing option. Depends-on FGAP-090 (wire the hooks) + FEAT-007 (JIT skills as corpus).

gaps.title: initProject auto-copies defaults/schemas + defaults/blocks at init — violates DEC-0011 (ship no defaults) and is in tension with DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral).
gaps.description: initProject (index.ts:255-295) unconditionally copies defaults/schemas/* (14 schemas) + defaults/blocks/* (9 blocks) into a fresh substrate — imposing a conception at init. DEC-0011 (enacted) states pi-context ships NO default schemas or block files and users install desired schemas; this is a direct contradiction. There are THREE package schema channels — schemas/ (framework-contract, bundled, read by loadConfig), defaults/ (init-seed), registry/ (install catalog) — with defaults/ a subset of registry/ and divergence between them, a standing maintenance hazard. Both defaults/ and registry/ currently carry inline-FK schemas, so a fresh adopter’s substrate is born DEC-0013-violating the moment they init/install (this is the unit-2.2 concern). Intended outcome: init imposes nothing; a fresh substrate carries only what the user declares or accepts from the suggestion catalog — i.e. the defaults/ auto-seed is removed and superseded by the onboarding-elicitation ceremony (sibling FGAP).

evidence.reference: initProject resolves defaults/ dir then unconditionally copies defaults/schemas/* and defaults/blocks/* into the substrate — the conception-imposition at init
the two copy loops (schemas then blocks) that seed a fixed vocabulary regardless of user intent
DEC-0011 (enacted): pi-context does not ship default schemas or block files; users install desired schemas — directly contradicted. DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutral tension.

gaps.impact: Imposes a fixed vocabulary on every fresh project, contradicting enacted DEC-0011 and undercutting DEC-0025. Compounds with the three-channel divergence (defaults vs registry) as silent drift, and with inline-FK in shipped schemas (born-violating substrate). Must resolve in concert with the onboarding-elicitation FGAP (the elicitation is the replacement) and unit 2.2 (channel reconciliation + edge-based schemas).
gaps.proposed_resolution: Stop imposing a conception at init; the onboarding-elicitation ceremony (sibling FGAP) is the intended replacement, and registry/ becomes the suggestion catalog rather than an auto-default. Channel reconciliation (the defaults/ vs registry/ relationship) and exact removal mechanics are intentionally NOT prescribed here (outcome-only filing per user directive) — sequenced with the onboarding FGAP + unit 2.2 at the user’s direction.

gaps.title: Samples catalog is not an enumerable/described/queryable registry — no tool lists the installable block kinds; discovery is filename-convention only.
gaps.description: The package registry/ folder (the samples catalog) is reached only by filename guess: installProject takes config.installed_schemas[] names and does path.join(registryRoot, schemas, name+.schema.json) existsSync (index.ts:323-377). There is no manifest/index and no tool that enumerates or describes what is installable. The installed-side discovery functions (availableSchemas/availableBlocks, project-sdk.ts) readdir the project SUBSTRATE (what is already installed), never the catalog. Per DEC-0014 the harness-confined main LLM has NO fs/read tools, so discovery MUST be a tool over a described registry, not a dir read. The raw material exists — every schema carries title + description (DEC-0023) — so a catalog is derivable but not materialized as a queryable surface. read-config (index.ts:884) is the existing exemplar of a queryable registry behind a tool (it returns the config registries as structured JSON); the samples catalog is the missing analog. Intended outcome: a samples catalog that is enumerable + described + queryable via a discovery tool (list installable kinds with title/description/shape), so onboarding can offer them and any LLM can answer what can I add without reading a directory.

evidence.reference: installProject reaches registry/ by name-by-name existsSync against config.installed_schemas — no enumeration, no manifest, no describe
availableSchemas/availableBlocks readdir the installed substrate (what is installed), not the package samples catalog (what is installable)
read-config is the queryable-registry-via-tool exemplar (config registries as structured JSON) to extend to the samples catalog
DEC-0014 (harness-confined main LLM has no fs tools — discovery must be tool-mediated) + DEC-0023 (schema title/description make a catalog derivable) + DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral)

gaps.impact: Onboarding cannot offer samples it cannot enumerate; there is zero discovery of the installable set (a user must know schema names a priori to declare them). The queryable-registry-via-tool model (DEC-0014) is realized only for config, absent for the samples catalog. Substrate that the onboarding-elicitation gap (sibling) depends on.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Materialize the samples catalog as an enumerable, described, queryable registry behind a discovery tool (list installable kinds with title/description/shape), the analog of read-config for the catalog. Implementation specifics intentionally NOT prescribed (outcome-only per user directive); the implementer MUST first survey the pi-sdk surface + prior art in other pi extensions for canonical best-practice registry/discovery shapes and adopt the exemplar. Distinct from the onboarding ceremony (which depends on it) and from FGAP-056 (hardcoded block types) though same derive-from-registry theme.

gaps.title: status_buckets falls back to a hardcoded default status vocabulary (STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS) — DEC-0025 smell (source ships vocabulary).
gaps.description: resolveStatusVocabulary (roadmap-plan.ts:111) returns {...STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS, ...(config.status_buckets ?? {})} — a project with no status_buckets inherits a status vocabulary baked into source. Per DEC-0025 (the framework source ships zero vocabulary commitment; vocabulary derives from config/schemas) this is a latent violation, directly parallel to FGAP-056 (PROJECT_BLOCK_TYPES hardcodes block kinds). Surfaced while deriving the no-optional / degenerate-default registry semantics: unlike layers (whose degenerate default = 1 implicit layer is legitimate) status_buckets degenerate default is a source-shipped vocabulary, not a structural fallback. Intended outcome: status vocabulary derives from config (or the samples-catalog default), not a source constant; an empty config yields genuine none or a catalog-supplied default, never a source-baked vocabulary.

evidence.reference: resolveStatusVocabulary merges config.status_buckets over the source constant STATUS_VOCABULARY_DEFAULTS — source ships a status vocabulary
DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral: source ships zero vocabulary) — contradicted by a shipped status vocabulary

gaps.impact: A fresh adopter inherits pi-context status vocabulary unbidden; DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutrality is incomplete for status. Same derive-from-registry theme as FGAP-056 (hardcoded block kinds); both are source-shipped-vocabulary smells to resolve via the samples-catalog/config default mechanism.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Source the status vocabulary from config / the samples-catalog default rather than a source constant; align with whatever default mechanism the samples catalog establishes. Implementation specifics intentionally NOT prescribed (outcome-only); coordinate with FGAP-056 (same theme) and the samples-catalog default semantics.

gaps.title: Lens composition cannot express edge-traversal rollups — only field-filtered item-set unions
gaps.description: The lens `composition` kind (config.schema.json lenses[].members) unions members that are each either a sub-lens reference or a {from, where} field-equality item-filter; resolveComposition (project-context.ts) walks those members and unions the resulting item-sets. There is NO lens construct for walking closure-table edges to assemble a hierarchical/containment rollup (e.g. feature_contains_story then story_contains_task, or phase→task). Surfaced in unit 2.2 (DEC-0037 packaged conception, commits b8a9d7e B0 / a4c5257 B4): the intended `work-rollup` starter lens (feature→story→task containment projection) could not be authored because the composition shape cannot express edge-traversal and lenses[] had zero precedent to validate an edge-rollup shape against. Shipped the 2 field-derived status lenses (tasks-by-status, gaps-by-status) and deferred work-rollup. Note: the edge-traversal PRIMITIVES already exist (walkDescendants / edgesForLens / walkLensDescendants in project-context.ts + lens-view.ts) — the gap is that the lens-SPEC has no construct that consumes them for rollup projection. Intended outcome: a lens construct expressing edge-traversal rollups (walk one or more relation_types to a max_depth; nest/group items by the traversal), so containment hierarchies become first-class lens projections — completing DEC-0037 starter-lens coverage for composite/rollup kinds.

evidence.reference: lenses[] composition members = sub-lens ref OR {from, where} field-equality filter; no edge-traversal/relation_type-walk member shape
resolveComposition unions field-filtered member item-sets; walkDescendants/edgesForLens edge-traversal primitives exist but are not consumable from a lens spec for rollup projection
lenses[] ships 2 field-derived status lenses; work-rollup edge-traversal lens absent (deferred at unit 2.2 B0)

DEC-0037 (each sample packaged with a starter lens) — incomplete for composite/rollup kinds without an edge-traversal lens construct; DEC-0013 (relations are edges — the surface a rollup lens would traverse)

gaps.impact: Containment/rollup projections — the dominant show-a-feature's-full-work-tree and show-a-phase's-tasks views — are not expressible as lenses. DEC-0037 starter-lens coverage is field-derived-only; composite kinds (feature/phase) get no rollup projection. Blocks authoring the work-rollup sample lens.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Add a lens construct that expresses edge-traversal rollups (walk declared relation_type(s) to a max_depth from a root block, nesting/grouping items by the traversal), backed by the existing walkDescendants/edgesForLens primitives. Implementation specifics intentionally NOT prescribed (outcome-only); the implementer should survey the existing closure-table traversal surface (project-context.ts walkDescendants/edgesForLens, lens-view.ts walkLensDescendants) + reuse them rather than add a parallel traversal. Once landed, author the deferred work-rollup sample lens (feature_contains_story → story_contains_task) in samples/conception.json.

gaps.title: block_kind canonical_id rename requires a file/data_path/array_key/schema_path cascade — unsupported by renameCanonicalId (deferred from FGAP-060/unit 2.3)
gaps.description: The FGAP-060 renameCanonicalId engine (commit 33b76a7, unit 2.3) supports 4 canonical_id kinds (item, relation_type, lens, layer) and THROWS on kind=block_kind. block_kind canonical_id rename is entangled with the block data-file basename, data_path, array_key, and schema_path: buildIdIndex (project-sdk.ts) resolves loc.block by the data-file basename, while config.invariants[].block / lenses[].target / lenses[].targets[] / lenses[].members[].from / hierarchy[].parent_block / hierarchy[].child_block reference the block_kind canonical_id. Renaming the canonical_id alone (config references only) produces canonical_id != basename, breaking buildIdIndex prefix-vs-block invariant + invariant/lens block-resolution. A correct block_kind rename is a coupled filesystem cascade: rename <old>.json -> <new>.json + schemas/<old>.schema.json -> schemas/<new>.schema.json + config.block_kinds[].{canonical_id,data_path,array_key,schema_path} + every config reference, keeping canonical_id == basename. Rare by design (DEC-0023: display_name is the relabel surface; block_kind canonical_id rename is near-never for settled conceptions). Intended outcome: extend renameCanonicalId with a block_kind cascade path (file + schema + array_key + config references, atomic) OR canonicalize block_kind canonical_id immutability post-authoring.

evidence.reference: renameCanonicalId throws on kind=block_kind (unsupported — filesystem cascade)
buildIdIndex resolves loc.block by data-file basename, decoupled from config.block_kinds[].canonical_id — the coupling a block_kind rename must keep consistent
DEC-0035 names block_kind canonical_id as a primary-key the engine serves; DEC-0023 makes display_name the cheap relabel surface; DEC-0013 edge model

gaps.impact: A block_kind canonical_id cannot be renamed through tooling; if ever needed it is a manual multi-file cascade. Low practical impact — display_name handles relabeling and block_kind canonical_id rename is rare for settled conceptions.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Either extend renameCanonicalId with a block_kind path that performs the data-file + schema-file + array_key + config-reference cascade atomically, OR canonicalize block_kind canonical_id immutability post-authoring (display_name only). Implementation specifics NOT prescribed (outcome-only); survey the data_path/array_key/schema_path/basename coupling first.

gaps.title: Full-state derivation capability (currentState) — derive the project's complete state from the substrate as an extension capability exposed dual-surface; do not hand-maintain it
gaps.description: Zero-loss (DEC-0040, as amended 2026-05-21) requires the project's CURRENT STATE (focus / per-arc status / in-flight / next-action / blocked) to be a pure function of the SUBSTRATE — an EXTENSION capability, exposed via the dual surface (Pi tool + scripts/orchestrator/*) to BOTH the Pi runtime and the Claude Code harness (consumed exactly as filing is). FGAP-059 derives only what-is-next. UN-CONFLATION (DEC-0040 amendment): the earlier 'projection engine that regenerates MEMORY.md/HANDOFF/Claude-tasks/plan' framing conflated two layers — those are CLAUDE CODE HARNESS-owned files, not extension projections; the extension's job is to EXPOSE currentState, and the harness APPLIES it to refresh its own files (a harness workflow/skill, not extension code). CORE LANDED: sub-unit 2.6A (commit 0cbd676) shipped currentState(cwd) -> { focus, inFlight, nextActions, blocked } + the context-current-state Pi tool + scripts/orchestrator/current-state.ts (dual surface), reusing projectState + roadmap-plan topoSort/resolveStatusVocabulary; verified on the live .project. REMAINING: atomic-next refinement — v1 ranks open gaps by priority+id + unblocked tasks by topo, but is not active-arc-aware (it surfaces all open P1 gaps, not 'the one next thing'); enrich to weight the active arc / in-flight phase. Subsumes FGAP-059.

evidence.reference: projectState derives partial state; no full focus/next/in-flight derivation; validateProject does not project to consuming surfaces
TASK-043 in-progress (superseded in-place migration) + TASK-044 planned (rename engine actually delivered) — stored state contradicts reality
FGAP-059 atomic-next derives only next-action, not full state

gaps.impact: State drift = loss, violating the zero-loss goal. A fresh session is misled before it reads anything else: MEMORY.md auto-loads a wrong + truncated focus. Execution truth survives only in one hand-maintained gitignored file (HANDOFF).
gaps.proposed_resolution: currentState(cwd) deriving complete state from the substrate, exposed dual-surface (Pi tool + orchestrator script) — usable from the Claude Code harness as filing is; the harness applies it to refresh its own working-memory files (harness-side, not extension-written, per DEC-0040 amendment). Core delivered in sub-unit 2.6A. Remaining (outcome-only): refine atomic-next to be active-arc/in-flight-aware rather than priority+id over all open gaps. Reuse projectState + roadmap-plan topo/status; do not parallel them. Extends FGAP-059.

gaps.title: Unit-of-work closure produces no enforced substrate record (DEC-0018/0020 unenforced) + validateProject lacks cross-block status-drift checks
gaps.description: Two coupled enforcement holes that let process/state leak out of the substrate (zero-loss goal, user 2026-05-21). (1) DEC-0020 mandates every work-unit produce typed substrate blocks (TASK/VER); DEC-0018 mandates runtime-demo + adversarial verification per step. Both are unenforced: this session three substantial units (2.1b config-declared invariants, 2.2 packaged conception, 2.3 rename engine) landed as commits + Claude Code tasks + HANDOFF prose but produced NO TASK/VER blocks (units 2.1b/2.2 have no task at all; 2.3 = TASK-044 left planned). The process-of-work is therefore not reconstructable from the substrate alone. (2) validateProject has no cross-block status-consistency check: nothing flags that TASK-044 is planned while its FGAP-060 is closed and the rename engine is shipped, or that TASK-043 is in-progress for a DEC-0036-superseded approach. The substrate cannot self-detect the lying-state condition. Intended outcome: (a) a unit-closure gate — a unit is not done until its TASK + VER (+ any DEC) are filed; (b) validateProject cross-block status-drift diagnostics (task vs its referenced gap/feature status; completed-task vs verification; task describing superseded work) so the substrate surfaces its own drift.

evidence.reference: DEC-0020 (units produce typed substrate blocks) + DEC-0018 (verify per step) — mandated but unenforced; units 2.1b/2.2/2.3 violated both
TASK-044 planned while FGAP-060 closed + engine shipped; TASK-043 in-progress for superseded in-place migration — undetected drift
validateProject has edge-integrity + config-invariants but no cross-block status-consistency (task<->gap/feature/verification) checks

gaps.impact: Process-of-work leaks out of the substrate (zero-loss violation); the substrate cannot self-detect status drift, so stale state goes silently uncaught until a human audit. Feeds the state-derivation gap (FGAP-072): derived state is only as honest as the statuses it reads.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Enforce unit-closure substrate records (TASK+VER per unit, per DEC-0018/0020) + add validateProject cross-block status-consistency diagnostics. Implementation specifics NOT prescribed (outcome-only). Lands under DEC-0040; statuses it validates feed FGAP-072 state derivation.

gaps.title: Residual pi-project-era 'project' naming debt across pi-context source/surface — package is pi-context + dir .context (DEC-0029) + surface /context, but source identifiers, files, Pi tools, and the command still say project
gaps.description: The 2026-05-08 rebuild renamed the package pi-project -> pi-context; DEC-0029 set the substrate dir to .context and named a forthcoming /context subcommand surface; FGAP-066 designs /context init onboarding. But the SOURCE still carries pi-project-era project naming throughout (~1079 occurrences in packages/pi-context/src): 6 project-*.ts files (project-sdk, project-context, project-dir + tests), 3 package.json subpath exports (./project-sdk, ./project-context, ./project-dir), ~13 Pi tool names + the /project command + customTypes (project-status, project-init, project-validate, project-validate-relations, project-edges-for-lens, project-walk-descendants, project-roadmap-load/render/validate/list, project-add-work, project-lens-curate), 12 exported project* identifiers (projectState, projectRoot, projectDir, validateProject, ...), and cross-package consumers in pi-jit-agents / pi-workflows / pi-behavior-monitors that import the ./project-* subpaths. This is INTERNAL-NAMING inconsistency debt, NOT a DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutrality violation: DEC-0025 governs the user-substrate CONCEPTION vocabulary (block kinds / relation types / statuses, derived from config), whereas project-sdk / projectState / /project are the framework own module + tool identifiers. The substrate-DIR name (.project via config.root) is config-data per DEC-0015 (resolveContextDir; the .project literal already removed from source per FGAP-035) and flips to .context at cutover (DEC-0029) — a separate concern from this source-identifier debt. Intended outcome: rename the project->context source identifier surface (project-sdk.ts -> context-sdk.ts; project-context.ts / project-dir.ts -> context equivalents; projectState/projectRoot/projectDir/validateProject -> context or substrate equivalents; /project -> /context command; project-* -> context-* Pi tools; ./project-* -> ./context-* subpath exports; + every cross-package consumer import + tests) so code matches the package / dir / surface name. OPEN sub-decision (settle first, worth a DEC): the target-naming scheme — e.g. project-context.ts is itself awkward (context-context); candidates context-core / context-substrate; projectRoot/projectDir -> contextRoot/contextDir; validateProject -> validateContext or validateSubstrate. Mechanism: a source-wide codemod across all four packages + tool/command registrations + package.json subpaths + consumer imports + tests — DISTINCT from the FGAP-060 renameCanonicalId engine (which renames SUBSTRATE canonical_ids, not source code). Until this FGAP it was captured only as HANDOFF re-derivation step-4 prose + the plan file (a DEC-0040 single-source violation this filing corrects). [ORGANIZING POINTER 2026-05-22] The substrate-dir-name concern this gap scopes out as a separate concern is now filed as FGAP-094 (shipped .project default in samples/conception.json). Residual-project cluster = FGAP-074 (source/surface identifier naming) + FGAP-094 (shipped substrate-dir default) + DEC-0036 step-4 (/project -> /context command rename, within 074 scope) + DEC-0036 step-5 (.project -> .context dir cutover).

evidence.reference: SDK named project-sdk despite pi-context package / .context dir / /context surface; exports projectState/validateProject/buildIdIndex etc.
./project-sdk, ./project-context, ./project-dir subpath exports (consumed cross-package) carry the old name
~13 project-* Pi tool names + /project command + customTypes

DEC-0029 (.context dir + forthcoming /context surface) — code lags it; DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral) is a DISTINCT concern (user vocab, not framework identifiers); DEC-0040 (single-source) — this rename was prose-only until now

gaps.impact: Code/surface name mismatch (pi-context package + .context dir + /context surface vs project-* source) confuses adopters and every fresh LLM, and the /context surface (FGAP-026 Phase 6 + FGAP-066 onboarding) cannot land cleanly without it. Was untracked in substrate (HANDOFF/plan prose only) — a DEC-0040 violation.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Settle the target-naming scheme (open sub-decision; candidate a DEC), then codemod the project->context source identifier surface (files / exports / Pi tools / command / package subpaths / cross-package consumers / tests) to match package + dir + surface. Outcome-only; not the FGAP-060 substrate canonical_id engine (this is source code). Sequenced as re-derivation step 4 (extension refactor), after step 3 re-populate + before/with cutover (step 5) — exact sequencing the user's call.

gaps.title: No edge-write Pi tool — in-pi agents cannot create DEC-0013 relations through the extension tool surface
gaps.description: For any harness-confined in-pi agent (workflow step, monitor, dispatched agent, or a tools-only Pi on a fresh install) restricted to extension-provided tools, there is no registered Pi tool to create a DEC-0013 relation (edge). relations.json is a bare top-level JSON array of {parent, child, relation_type, ordinal?}; the generic append-block-item / update-block-item tools target data[arrayKey] and therefore cannot append to a bare array (empirically: appendToBlock against relations throws 'must be array'). Edge creation exists only as the library writeRelations (project-context.ts) plus the orchestrator CLI (scripts/orchestrator/migrate-canonical-id.ts) plus the rename engine internals — none exposed as a Pi tool. So the foundational primitive of the whole model (an inter-item relationship) is unreachable through the tool surface. Intended outcome: an edge-write Pi tool, the dual-surface twin of writeRelations.

evidence.reference: tool enumeration: relation tools are all read-only (project-validate-relations, project-edges-for-lens, project-walk-descendants, walk-ancestors, find-references); no edge-write / append-relation tool is registered
writeRelations exists as a library surface (consumed by the rename-canonical-id engine + orchestrator migrate-canonical-id.ts) but has no pi.registerTool twin

relations is a bare top-level JSON array (no array_key), so generic append/update-block-item (which operate on object[arrayKey]) structurally cannot write edges; empirical probe: appendToBlock on relations fails 'must be array'
DEC-0013 (edges are THE inter-item relationship surface; FK-as-field forbidden) + DEC-0019/0020 (every substrate op = library + Pi tool + CLI script as a unit) — the Pi-tool leg is the missing one

gaps.impact: In-pi agents cannot author relationships: the .context re-population (DEC-0036 step 3), any tool-only consumer, and any in-pi workflow/monitor that needs to relate items are all blocked from the core primitive. The DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface is broken precisely at DEC-0013, the model's foundation. Sharpest of the three write-twin gaps — not even a degenerate path exists (append-block-item structurally rejects the bare-array shape; only write-block whole-file replace could blind-overwrite relations.json, unvalidated).
gaps.proposed_resolution: Register an edge-write Pi tool as the dual-surface twin of writeRelations — append a single edge {parent, child, relation_type, ordinal?} (and/or a bulk variant), routed through the canonical write path with the same atomicity (tmp+rename) and DispatchContext attestation as the item-CRUD tools. Guard semantics (relation_type registered in config.relation_types; endpoints resolve via buildIdIndex; cycle detection) either enforced at write or deferred to validateProject — implementer to choose per existing precedent (validateRelations already centralizes these checks). Implementation specifics outcome-only; survey the pi-sdk surface + sibling pi extensions for the canonical tool shape. DEC-0013 + DEC-0019/0020.

gaps.title: No config-write Pi tool — in-pi agents cannot author or amend config.json (framework vocabulary)
gaps.description: There is no registered Pi tool to author or amend config.json — the framework vocabulary: block_kinds, relation_types, lenses, invariants, status_buckets, display_strings, layers, installed_schemas/blocks. read-config is read-only; project-init does not scaffold config.json at all (defaults/ ships neither config.json nor config.schema.json — empirical: a fresh-init read-config returns null and validateProject is vacuously clean because there is nothing to validate). writeConfig exists as a library surface (project-context.ts, consumed by the orchestrator + the rename engine) with no pi.registerTool twin. So a tools-only in-pi agent cannot register a block_kind / relation_type / lens / invariant, nor set a config.status_buckets override (DEC-0025) — the entire config-driven machinery (DEC-0015/0025) is unestablishable and unrevisable from inside Pi.

evidence.reference: only read-config registered (read-only); no write-config / amend-config tool
writeConfig library surface exists (orchestrator + rename engine consume it); no Pi-tool twin
project-init scaffold omits config.json + config.schema.json; empirical fresh-init: read-config = null, validateProject vacuously clean

DEC-0015 (config-driven substrate dir + vocabulary) + DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral; config.status_buckets override) + DEC-0019/0020 (dual-surface) + DEC-0038 (onboarding) — the Pi-tool twin is missing

gaps.impact: Framework vocabulary cannot be created or revised via tools; onboarding (DEC-0038) — which must elicit and write config — has no write surface; config-dependent tools (project-validate, context-current-state, lenses, roadmap, gather-execution-context) degrade to empty/vacuous on any substrate an in-pi agent provisioned. Blocks the consumption arc (FGAP-068 onboarding rewire) from completing on the tool side.
gaps.proposed_resolution: Register a config-write Pi tool, the dual-surface twin of writeConfig — AJV-validated against config.schema.json, supporting scoped amendments (add or replace a single relation_type / block_kind / lens / invariant / status_bucket entry) in addition to whole-config write, with atomic write + DispatchContext attestation.
